zh-tw.title: 食物笑話精選
zh-tw.description: 從諧音梗店名到夜市對話，收錄各種讓人邊笑邊餓的台味食物笑話

en.title: Food Jokes Collection
en.description: From pun-filled shop names to night market banter — bite-sized food jokes that'll make you laugh and crave a snack

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音梗店名文化參考

zh-tw.content:
朋友開了間飲料店叫「可不可」。
我問：「為什麼取這個名字？」
他：「客人問可不可以打折，我可以理直氣壯回他 — 不可以。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在 LINE 群組分享，搭配抱怨被店家拒絕折扣的話題
variations: 也可以改成早餐店版本：店名「加不加」，客人問加蛋免錢嗎，老闆回 — 不加

en.content:
My friend opened a drink shop called "Can or Can't."
I asked why.
Him: "When customers ask if they can get a discount, the answer is right there in the name — they can't."
en.editorNote: Share in chats about petty business names
variations: Breakfast shop version: name it "Yes or No." Customer asks if eggs are free. Owner: "No."

items.sourceNote: 端午節 粽子擬人格式 — 2026-05-02 research log seed

zh-tw.content:
粽子對著人說：
「我卸下外衣以後，照樣光鮮亮麗。
不像你 — 一張嘴，就露餡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節前後在 IG 限動發，配粽子照超應景
variations: 也可以加一句：「你還是先把嘴閉好再說話吧」
對主管版本：「主管的承諾跟粽子一樣 — 包得很漂亮，打開全是餡」

en.content:
A zongzi says to a person:
"When I take off my wrap, I still look good.
Unlike you — open your mouth, and the filling spills out."
en.editorNote: Perfect for Dragon Boat Festival posts with a zongzi photo
variations: Boss version: a manager's promise is just like a zongzi — wrapped beautifully, but open it up and it's all filler

items.sourceNote: 珍奶 厭世格式 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼珍珠奶茶這麼受歡迎？
因為它有奶、有茶、有珍珠 — 比很多人的人生豐富多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一上班 LINE 群組吐槽用
variations: 也可以接：「至少珍珠每一顆都很扎實，不像我的人生鬆散」

en.content:
Why is bubble tea so popular?
It's got milk, tea, and pearls — already richer than most people's lives.
en.editorNote: Monday morning group chat material
variations: Add: "At least every pearl is solid — unlike my life choices."

items.sourceNote: 夜市食物擬人 — original

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「為什麼臭豆腐越臭越好吃？」
我：「因為它放下了包袱，做自己。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 可以接著聊「人生何嘗不是一塊臭豆腐」
variations: 延伸版：「臭豆腐告訴我們，被討厭也能很搶手」

en.content:
Friend: "Why does stinky tofu taste better the smellier it gets?"
Me: "Because it stopped caring what people think."
en.editorNote: Use as a follow-up to "life is just a piece of stinky tofu"
variations: Extension: "Stinky tofu teaches us — you can be polarizing and still be in demand."

items.sourceNote: 牛肉麵 厭世上班族 cross — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼牛肉麵的湯特別香？
因為它熬了一整夜 —
跟剛下班的我一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合加班後在限動發牛肉麵照
variations: 也可以接：「差別是它越熬越香，我越熬越爛」

en.content:
Why is beef noodle soup so flavorful?
It simmered all night —
same as me after work.
en.editorNote: Pair with a late-night noodle photo on stories
variations: Extension: "The difference is it gets better, I get worse."

items.sourceNote: 早餐店日常對話 — original

zh-tw.content:
早餐店阿姨：「妹妹今天加蛋還是加培根？」
我：「都加。」
阿姨：「昨天熬夜了喔？」
我：「妳怎麼看出來的？」
阿姨：「沒睡飽的人才會用兩種蛋白質補回去。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班族日常觀察，適合限動配蛋餅照
variations: 結尾改成：阿姨默默多送一杯豆漿，說「撐住」

en.content:
Breakfast shop auntie: "Egg or bacon today, sweetie?"
Me: "Both, please."
Auntie: "Pulled an all-nighter, didn't you?"
Me: "How can you tell?"
Auntie: "Only sleep-deprived people order two kinds of protein to compensate."
en.editorNote: Office worker observational humor — pair with an egg pancake photo
variations: Ending alt: Auntie quietly throws in a free soy milk and says, "Hang in there."

items.sourceNote: 雞排 考試文化 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼考完試一定要吃雞排？
因為書讀進去要靠記憶力，
雞排吃進去只要靠胃容量 —
至少胃比大腦聽話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 考試季學生群組分享必備
variations: 畢業季版本：「至少這四年，我的胃從沒讓我失望」

en.content:
Why do students always eat fried chicken after exams?
Memorizing facts depends on your brain.
Eating chicken just depends on your stomach —
and at least the stomach actually does what you tell it.
en.editorNote: Group chat fuel during finals week
variations: Graduation version: "In four years of college, my stomach never let me down."

items.sourceNote: 滷肉飯 療癒 — original

zh-tw.content:
滷肉飯是台灣人的心靈療癒。
不管那天再怎麼難熬，
看到那碗淋上滷汁的飯 —
你就會原諒自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上厭世時 po 文用
variations: 也可以結尾改：「然後決定明天再厭世一次」

en.content:
Braised pork rice is Taiwan's soul food.
No matter how rough the day was,
the moment you see the gravy hit the rice —
you forgive yourself.
en.editorNote: Sunday-night post when the Monday dread sets in
variations: Alt ending: "…and then decide to dread tomorrow all over again."

items.sourceNote: 餃子 諧音 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼餃子永遠不會孤單？

因為它總是「成雙成對」的下水餃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 情人節單身者反擊用，記得面無表情念出來
variations: 對單身者版本：「我跟餃子一樣，都是被一鍋同類包圍著卻覺得很孤單」

en.content:
Why are dumplings never lonely?

They always go into the pot in pairs — and never solo.
en.editorNote: Single-person comeback for Valentine's — deliver with a straight face
variations: Single-person version: "I'm like a dumpling — surrounded by my own kind in a pot, and still feel alone."

items.sourceNote: 鶴茶樓 諧音店名 文化

zh-tw.content:
經過一間飲料店叫「鶴茶樓」。
我跟朋友說：「來喝茶嘍！」
朋友：「我不渴。」
我：「沒關係，這店名你只要念出口，就等於下單了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣諧音店名文化代表，可搭配各種台味店名介紹
variations: 也可以加上「八曜和茶」「吾燕煮」等其他諧音店名延伸

en.content:
We passed a drink shop literally called "Crane Tea House" — but in Mandarin it sounds exactly like "let's go drink tea!"
The trick is: if you say its name out loud, you've already placed an order.
en.editorNote: Showcase of Taiwan's pun-shop-name culture — works as an intro to any "funny shop names" thread
variations: Extend with other classic pun shop names from Taiwan's homophone-loving food scene

items.sourceNote: 火鍋 天氣連結 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼下雨天大家都想吃火鍋？

因為人生已經夠濕了，
至少嘴巴裡的東西要熱的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 梅雨季 LINE 群組約飯用
variations: 冬天版本：「天氣已經夠冷，至少胃要先給自己暖一下」

en.content:
Why does everyone crave hot pot on rainy days?

Life is already wet enough —
at least keep your mouth warm.
en.editorNote: Use this to drum up rainy-day group dinners
variations: Winter version: "Weather's cold enough — at least your stomach should warm itself first."

items.sourceNote: 上班族 便當 厭世 — original

zh-tw.content:
上班族的便當有三種：
今天買的、昨天剩的、
以及「沒時間吃」的。

而第三種，永遠是賣得最好的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合午休群組吐槽用
variations: 也可以延伸：「第四種叫做『放在抽屜裡發現時已長毛』的」

en.content:
There are three kinds of office worker lunchbox:
today's, yesterday's leftovers,
and the "no time to eat it" one.

The third type is always the bestseller.
en.editorNote: Lunch-break group chat fuel
variations: Extension: "The fourth kind is the one you find in your drawer two weeks later, growing fur."

items.sourceNote: 鹹酥雞 攤販對話 — original

zh-tw.content:
鹹酥雞攤老闆：「九層塔要嗎？」
我：「要。」
老闆：「辣嗎？」
我：「微辣。」
老闆：「胡椒？」
我：「OK。」
老闆：「再加 30 元。」
我：「為什麼？」
老闆：「靈魂的代價。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 下班買鹹酥雞時 IG 限動配照片用
variations: 也可以結尾改：老闆嘆氣說「現在連靈魂都漲價了」

en.content:
Fried chicken vendor: "Basil?"
Me: "Yes."
Vendor: "Spicy?"
Me: "Mild."
Vendor: "Pepper?"
Me: "Sure."
Vendor: "That'll be thirty more."
Me: "For what?"
Vendor: "The price of soul."
en.editorNote: Caption for late-night street food photos
variations: Alt ending: vendor sighs — "Even soul has inflation these days."

items.sourceNote: 端午節 粽子派系 上班族 cross — 2026-05-02 research log

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「你今年端午吃哪一派的粽子？」
我：「中立派。」
同事：「中立派是什麼？」
我：「誰先送到我家，誰就是我心目中的正統。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節前一週，用來終結南北粽戰爭
variations: 也可以接：「我發現嘴巴塞滿粽子的時候，立場其實很容易改變」

en.content:
Coworker: "Which side are you on for zongzi this year — northern or southern?"
Me: "Neutral."
Coworker: "What does neutral mean?"
Me: "Whoever delivers to my house first wins my heart."
en.editorNote: Use the week before Dragon Boat Festival to end the regional zongzi war
variations: Add: "Turns out, with a mouthful of zongzi, your political stance becomes very flexible."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名文化 — 火鍋店 豪啃 延伸

zh-tw.content:
看到一間火鍋店叫「豪啃」。
朋友：「為什麼叫這個名字？」
我：「老闆說 — 來這吃飯不能優雅，要『好啃』，啃到桌子都搖。」
朋友：「那啃完呢？」
我：「啃完換你被帳單啃。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 聚餐前在群組分享，預告錢包要陣亡
variations: 也可以接：「啃完發現薪水就在剛剛那一鍋裡蒸發了」
對情侶版本：「兩個人的浪漫，從互相啃對方的肉片開始」

en.content:
Saw a hot pot place called "Hao Ken" — a pun that means "good to gnaw on."
Friend: "Why that name?"
Me: "The owner says you can't be polite here. You gnaw until the table shakes."
Friend: "And after?"
Me: "After, the bill gnaws on you."
en.editorNote: Drop in the group chat before a hot pot dinner — pre-mourn your wallet
variations: Add: "Turns out my paycheck evaporated in the broth right there."
Couple version: "Romance starts with two people stealing meat slices off each other's chopsticks."

items.sourceNote: 外送 上班族 厭世 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼上班族每天都在點外送？

因為打開冰箱要面對的，
是過期的食材跟過期的自己。
至少外送員按門鈴的時候 —
還有人記得你住這裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週三晚上厭世點外送時 IG 限動配照片
variations: 也可以結尾改：「外送員是這禮拜唯一準時赴約的人」
延伸：「冰箱不是儲藏室，是我意志力的墳場」

en.content:
Why do office workers order delivery every single day?

Because opening the fridge means facing
expired ingredients and an expired version of yourself.
At least when the delivery rider rings the bell —
someone still remembers you live here.
en.editorNote: Wednesday-night burnout caption — pair with a delivery bag photo
variations: Alt ending: "The delivery rider is the only person who showed up on time this week."
Extension: "My fridge isn't storage — it's a graveyard for my willpower."

items.sourceNote: 豆花 阿嬤 對話 — original

zh-tw.content:
豆花阿嬤：「妹妹要加什麼料？」
我：「珍珠、紅豆、花生、芋圓、粉條都加。」
阿嬤：「妹妹是想吃豆花，還是想埋葬豆花？」
我：「我想被埋葬。」
阿嬤默默又多加了一勺糖水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在 IG 限動配豆花照，標記「今天也想被甜的東西埋了」
variations: 結尾改：阿嬤說「年輕人別這樣，糖會壞牙也會壞心情」
也可以延伸：「她加的不是糖水，是我這週的續命液」

en.content:
Tofu pudding auntie: "What toppings, sweetie?"
Me: "Pearls, red beans, peanuts, taro balls, jelly noodles — all of them."
Auntie: "Are you eating tofu pudding, or burying it?"
Me: "I want to be buried."
She quietly added an extra ladle of syrup.
en.editorNote: Tofu pudding photo caption — "please bury me in something sweet today"
variations: Alt ending: Auntie shakes her head — "Kids these days. Sugar rots teeth and moods."
Extension: "That wasn't syrup, that was my life support for the week."

items.sourceNote: 麻辣鍋 友情 哲學 — original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼朋友一起吃麻辣鍋特別有感情？

因為你們會一起流汗、一起流淚、
一起搶最後一塊鴨血 —
然後一起跑廁所。

這種共患難的革命情感，
比血緣還親。
zh-tw.editorNote: 麻辣鍋聚餐後群組合照配文，可愛又有梗
variations: 也可以接：「不能一起跑廁所的，不算真朋友」
對同事版本：「跟同事吃完麻辣鍋的隔天，辦公室空氣都變得很有故事」

en.content:
Why does eating spicy hot pot with friends feel so bonding?

You sweat together, cry together,
fight over the last piece of duck blood —
and then run to the bathroom together.

That kind of shared suffering
is thicker than blood.
en.editorNote: Caption for the post-spicy-hot-pot group photo
variations: Add: "If you can't run to the toilet together, you're not real friends."
Coworker version: "The morning after spicy hot pot with coworkers, the office air has stories to tell."

items.sourceNote: 你好我吃一點 貓咪梗圖 跨國美食外交 — 2026 trend

zh-tw.content:
日本朋友來台灣旅遊，限動發了一桌小籠包。
我留言：「你好，我吃一點。」
他回：「可以啊，請進。」

三秒後，整個 Threads 的台灣人都在他底下排隊，
手裡都拿著筷子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 搭配貓咪拿筷子梗圖效果最好，外國朋友來台限動可直接複製貼上
variations: 韓國朋友版本：對方回「你好，把你筷子收回去」，立刻變成跨國魔法對決
結尾延伸：「我們不是貪吃，是熱情款待 — 自助式的那種」

en.content:
A Japanese friend visiting Taiwan posted a table of soup dumplings on his story.
I commented: "Hi, I'll have a little."
He replied: "Sure, come on in."

Three seconds later, every Taiwanese person on Threads was lined up under his post —
chopsticks already in hand.
en.editorNote: Pair with the cat-holding-chopsticks meme; perfect copy-paste reply for tourist food posts
variations: Korean version: they reply "Hi, put your chopsticks back" — instant international meme duel
Add: "We're not greedy — it's hospitality. The self-serve kind."

items.sourceNote: 2026 全台夜市懶人包 — 逢甲永遠有新東西延伸

zh-tw.content:
為什麼逢甲夜市每三個月就有新爆紅小吃？

因為老闆們知道，台灣人的記憶只有三個月，
胃容量倒是永遠在擴張。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末夜市行 IG 限動配排隊照
variations: 也可以接：「上次紅的那家現在已經變成下一家的招牌外帶袋了」
對減肥版本：「每出一個新口味，我的減肥計畫就重啟一次」

en.content:
Why does Fengjia Night Market produce a new viral snack every three months?

Because the vendors know Taiwanese memory only lasts three months —
but stomach capacity expands forever.
en.editorNote: Weekend night market story caption, paired with a queue photo
variations: Add: "Last month's hottest stall is now just a takeout bag at the next one."
Diet version: "Every new flavor reboots my diet plan from scratch."

items.sourceNote: 寧夏夜市 米其林必比登 必吃排隊文化 — 2026

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「寧夏夜市為什麼這麼多米其林推薦？」
我：「因為米其林評審也要排隊半小時，他們的怒氣需要一個出口。」
朋友：「所以給星星是發洩？」
我：「給推薦是復仇 — 讓更多人也來體驗一樣的痛苦。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 排隊夜市時 IG 限動配人潮照，自嘲一下
variations: 也可以接：「排到的那一刻，你的飢餓已經升級成信仰」
對觀光客版本：「不要問值不值得 — 排了就是值得」

en.content:
Friend: "Why does Ningxia Night Market have so many Michelin picks?"
Me: "Because Michelin inspectors also waited thirty minutes. Their rage needed somewhere to go."
Friend: "So a star is a vent?"
Me: "A recommendation is revenge — make more people suffer the same line."
en.editorNote: Caption for a packed-night-market photo on stories — self-deprecating
variations: Add: "By the time you reach the front, hunger has upgraded into faith."
Tourist version: "Don't ask if it's worth it — once you've waited, it's worth it."

items.sourceNote: 外帶咖啡 上班族 厭世 — 2026 original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼上班族每天早上都要外帶一杯咖啡？

不是為了提神 —
是為了讓手裡有個東西，
撐到走進辦公室那一刻。

咖啡只是道具，
撐下去才是劇本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上厭世限動，配咖啡杯照
variations: 也可以接：「真正提神的不是咖啡因，是還沒繳完的房租」
延伸：「咖啡涼了我也涼了，但行程不會涼」

en.content:
Why do office workers grab a takeaway coffee every morning?

Not for the caffeine —
for something to hold,
just to make it through the office door.

The coffee is a prop.
Survival is the script.
en.editorNote: Monday morning burnout caption with a coffee cup photo
variations: Add: "What actually wakes me up isn't caffeine — it's unpaid rent."
Extension: "The coffee gets cold, I get cold, the calendar stays hot."

items.sourceNote: 台式早餐 蛋餅 粉漿派 vs 脆皮派 — 2026 派系戰爭

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「你是粉漿派還脆皮派？」
我：「中立派。」
同事：「又是中立派？」
我：「上面要我選邊站，可是熱騰騰的兩種我都吃過 — 我中立的不是蛋餅，是我的胃。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早餐限動配蛋餅照，順便終止辦公室派系戰
variations: 也可以接：「真正的中立派 — 點兩份，一手一個」
對減肥版本：「我的中立來自我的腰圍，它已經沒有立場了」

en.content:
Coworker: "Are you team batter-style or team crispy egg pancake?"
Me: "Neutral."
Coworker: "Neutral again?"
Me: "They keep telling me to pick a side, but I've eaten both hot off the griddle — what's neutral isn't the pancake, it's my stomach."
en.editorNote: Monday breakfast caption with an egg pancake photo — ends the office faction war
variations: Add: "True neutrality — order both, one in each hand."
Diet version: "My neutrality comes from my waistline. It has no opinions left."

items.sourceNote: 2026 愚人節 獵奇食物 肯德基香菜蛋塔 全黃布丁 — 風味荒謬延伸

zh-tw.content:
現在的食物推陳出新太快。
香菜蛋塔、芋泥珍奶豬血糕、抹茶滷肉飯。

以前是「不要再加奇怪的東西到食物裡」，
現在是「不奇怪的話，我為什麼要排隊」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獵奇新品上市時 IG 限動配排隊照，自嘲消費者邏輯
variations: 也可以接：「我們不是在吃飯，是在驗收老闆今年的瘋狂額度」
延伸：「正常的東西已經不夠正常了」

en.content:
Food trends move way too fast these days.
Cilantro egg tarts, taro-bubble-tea pig blood cake, matcha braised pork rice.

It used to be "stop putting weird stuff in food."
Now it's "if it isn't weird, why am I lining up?"
en.editorNote: Caption for limited-edition cursed-food queue photos — mock our own logic
variations: Add: "We're not eating — we're auditing the owner's annual weirdness budget."
Extension: "Normal food doesn't feel normal anymore."

items.sourceNote: 便利商店 加熱便當 上班族 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
便利商店店員：「便當要加熱嗎？」
我：「要。」
店員：「90 秒可以嗎？」
我：「可以。」
店員：「那 90 秒之後 — 你還想加熱什麼？」
我：「我自己。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜超商買宵夜限動配便當照
variations: 結尾改成：「店員默默把熱可可推到我面前」
也可以接：「我跟便當一樣 — 都是被生活冷凍過的」

en.content:
Convenience store clerk: "Heat up your bento?"
Me: "Yes."
Clerk: "Ninety seconds okay?"
Me: "Sure."
Clerk: "After ninety seconds — anything else you want heated?"
Me: "Me."
en.editorNote: Late-night convenience store bento photo caption
variations: Alt ending: "The clerk quietly slid a hot cocoa across the counter."
Add: "Me and the bento — both straight out of life's freezer."

items.sourceNote: 你好我吃一點 跨國美食外交 延伸 — 阿嬤接待版

zh-tw.content:
外國朋友來家裡吃飯。
他：「我吃飽了。」
阿嬤：「再吃一碗。」
他：「真的飽了。」
阿嬤：「再吃一碗。」
他：「我快不行了。」
阿嬤：「那再喝湯。」

他後來在 Threads 發文：
「在台灣，吃飽的標準不是你決定的，是阿嬤決定的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 招待外國朋友時用，超應景配阿嬤端菜照
variations: 也可以接：「阿嬤的『再吃一碗』是台灣最堅定的承諾」
對長輩版本：「我們家阿嬤的愛是用碗計算的」

en.content:
A foreign friend came over for dinner.
Him: "I'm full."
Grandma: "Have one more bowl."
Him: "Really, I'm full."
Grandma: "One more bowl."
Him: "I might die."
Grandma: "Then drink some soup."

Later he posted on Threads:
"In Taiwan, you don't decide when you're full. Grandma does."
en.editorNote: Use when hosting foreign friends — pair with a grandma-serving-food photo
variations: Add: "Grandma's 'one more bowl' is the most ironclad promise in Taiwan."
Family version: "In our house, love is measured in bowls of rice."

items.sourceNote: 減肥 厭世 食物哲學 — 2026 original

zh-tw.content:
為什麼減肥這麼難？

因為食物在說「我愛你」，
體重計在說「閉嘴」。
而身體 —
從頭到尾都在挑邊站。

你猜它選哪邊？
選有油的那邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 減肥失敗週末限動配雞排或火鍋照
variations: 也可以接：「我的意志力跟珍奶的冰塊一樣 — 五分鐘就化了」
對節食版本：「節食三天，靈魂出竅一次」

en.content:
Why is dieting so hard?

Food says "I love you."
The scale says "shut up."
And your body —
is picking a side the whole time.

Guess which one it picks?
The one with grease on it.
en.editorNote: Failed-diet weekend caption with fried chicken or hot pot photo
variations: Add: "My willpower has the lifespan of bubble tea ice — five minutes, gone."
Fasting version: "Three days of fasting, one out-of-body experience."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音招牌文化 — 大盜陳 月老奶茶 戀愛的滋味 延伸

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「為什麼這家飲料店叫『戀愛的滋味』？」
我：「半糖。」
朋友：「那『暗戀的滋味』是什麼？」
我：「三分糖 — 你不主動講，他永遠不知道你想喝什麼。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 情人節前後限動配奶茶照，搭配愛情諧音梗最對味
variations: 也可以接：「全糖叫做熱戀，無糖叫做分手」
對單身版本：「我點微糖去冰 — 跟我的感情狀態一樣，剛好涼掉」

en.content:
Friend: "Why is this drink shop called 'Taste of Love'?"
Me: "Half sugar."
Friend: "And 'Taste of a Crush'?"
Me: "Thirty percent sugar — if you don't speak up, they'll never know what you want."
en.editorNote: Caption for milk tea photos around Valentine's — pun-shop romance vibes
variations: Add: "Full sugar is hot romance, no sugar is the breakup."
Single version: "I order light sugar, no ice — same vibe as my love life. Already cold."

items.sourceNote: 夜市 CP 值 骰子牛 重組肉 厭世 — 2026 trend

zh-tw.content:
為什麼夜市的骰子牛這麼貴？

因為它跟你的薪水一樣 —
看起來是一塊一塊，
仔細一看其實是重組的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末逛夜市看到天價小吃時 IG 限動配照片
variations: 也可以接：「重組肉吃下去重組你的金錢觀」
對房租版本：「我的存款也是重組的 — 各方拼湊，禁不起細看」

en.content:
Why is night market dice beef so expensive?

Because it's just like your paycheck —
looks like solid chunks at first,
but take a closer look and it's all reassembled bits.
en.editorNote: Weekend night market caption when prices hurt
variations: Add: "Reassembled meat reassembles your understanding of money."
Rent version: "My savings are reassembled too — patched together, can't survive close inspection."

items.sourceNote: 夜市 排隊 信仰 — 2026 original

zh-tw.content:
外國朋友問：「台灣人為什麼這麼愛排隊？」
我：「我們不是愛排隊。」
他：「那是什麼？」
我：「我們是怕沒排到的人，會在限動笑我們沒吃過。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 排隊夜市攤販照片限動配文，自嘲一下
variations: 也可以接：「排到那一刻，比中樂透還激動 — 因為樂透不會限量」
對 FOMO 版本：「不是肚子餓，是怕被時代拋棄」

en.content:
Foreign friend: "Why do Taiwanese people love lining up so much?"
Me: "We don't love lining up."
Him: "Then what is it?"
Me: "We're scared the people who didn't will roast us on stories for never trying it."
en.editorNote: Story caption for queue photos at viral stalls — self-deprecating
variations: Add: "Reaching the front feels better than winning the lottery — because lotteries don't run out."
FOMO version: "It's not hunger. It's fear of being left behind."

items.sourceNote: 媽媽 冰箱 剩菜 哲學 — 2026 original

zh-tw.content:
回家打開冰箱，問媽媽：「這碗滷肉放多久了？」
媽：「還能吃。」
我：「我問放多久。」
媽：「我說還能吃。」

後來我才明白 —
在媽媽的字典裡，
「還能吃」就是時間單位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 返鄉省親限動配冰箱照，全台灣孩子都有共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「媽媽的冰箱沒有保存期限，只有信仰期限」
對阿嬤版本：「阿嬤的冰箱裡，連 2003 年的記憶都還能吃」

en.content:
Got home, opened the fridge, asked Mom: "How long has this braised pork been in here?"
Mom: "Still edible."
Me: "I asked how long."
Mom: "I said still edible."

Later I realized —
in Mom's dictionary,
"still edible" is a unit of time.
en.editorNote: Caption for fridge photos when visiting family — every kid in Taiwan relates
variations: Add: "Mom's fridge has no expiration date, only an expiration of faith."
Grandma version: "In Grandma's fridge, even memories from 2003 are still edible."

items.sourceNote: 2026 母親節 餐廳訂位 排隊 限動 — 2026-05-10 應景

zh-tw.content:
母親節訂餐廳的流程：
上禮拜訂 — 滿了。
上上禮拜訂 — 滿了。
上個月訂 — 滿了。

媽：「沒關係，我們在家吃就好。」

這句話是全台灣最危險的句子，
比餐廳訂不到還可怕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節前一天 IG 限動配空蕩蕩餐廳訂位畫面
variations: 也可以接：「我媽說『不用啦』的時候，全家人神經都繃緊了」
對外送版本：「最後我們點外送 — 我媽默默收下，然後說『以後是我自己煮比較快』」

en.content:
How booking a Mother's Day restaurant goes:
Last week — fully booked.
Two weeks ago — fully booked.
Last month — fully booked.

Mom: "It's okay, we can just eat at home."

That sentence is the most dangerous one in Taiwan —
scarier than no reservation.
en.editorNote: Mother's Day eve story caption with empty booking screen
variations: Add: "When Mom says 'no need,' the whole family goes on high alert."
Delivery version: "Ended up ordering takeout. Mom accepted it quietly, then said 'next year I'll just cook — it's faster.'"

items.sourceNote: 夜市 加熱滷味 上班族 厭世 — 2026 trend

zh-tw.content:
為什麼下班後我都點加熱滷味？

因為它跟我一樣 —
冷掉了還能再被加熱一次，
加熱完還是原本的味道，
只是更鹹一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週四加班深夜限動配滷味照，配文有厭世感
variations: 也可以接：「滷味老闆問我要不要加辣，我說『生活已經夠辣了』」
延伸：「我跟豆乾一樣，被泡在現實裡，越泡越入味」

en.content:
Why do I always order heated braised snacks after work?

Because they're like me —
go cold, then get reheated,
and still taste the same after,
just a little saltier.
en.editorNote: Thursday late-night overtime caption with a braised snack photo
variations: Add: "The vendor asked if I wanted it spicy. I said, 'life is already spicy enough.'"
Extension: "I'm like a piece of dried tofu — the longer I soak in reality, the more flavor I lose."

items.sourceNote: 2026 雙北夜市爭霸 在地人 觀光客 — 排隊文化延伸

zh-tw.content:
觀光客：「在地人最推薦的夜市是哪間？」
我：「在地人不去夜市。」
觀光客：「為什麼？」
我：「因為我們知道排前面那 30 分鐘，可以去巷口阿姨那邊吃兩輪。」
觀光客：「那阿姨的店在哪？」
我：「我不能說，說了她明天就要排隊了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在地人帶外國朋友逛夜市時用，自嘲觀光潮對在地店家的影響
variations: 也可以接：「真正的在地名單，是不能上 IG 的那種」
對返鄉版本：「我家樓下那間，紅了之後我反而吃不到了」

en.content:
Tourist: "Which night market do locals actually recommend?"
Me: "Locals don't go to night markets."
Tourist: "Why not?"
Me: "Because we know that thirty-minute queue could be two full meals at the auntie around the corner."
Tourist: "Where's her stall?"
Me: "Can't tell you. If I do, she'll have a line by tomorrow."
en.editorNote: Use when showing foreign friends around night markets — self-deprecating take on tourism
variations: Add: "The real local list is the one that never makes it to Instagram."
Hometown version: "The shop downstairs from my place — once it went viral, I stopped getting in."

items.sourceNote: 母親節 媽媽手藝 vs 餐廳 — 2026 應景 original

zh-tw.content:
母親節帶媽去米其林餐廳。
媽吃完說：「還行。」
回家後我問：「真的還行而已？」
媽：「鹹度不對、火候不夠、擺盤太少飯。」
我：「那妳怎麼當下說還行？」
媽：「人家小孩特地請的，要給面子啊。」

我才知道 —
媽媽的『還行』，
是全世界最貴的禮貌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節後 IG 限動配餐廳照，超有畫面感
variations: 也可以接：「下次直接帶她回家吃她自己煮的 — 至少她會多誇兩句」
對阿嬤版本：「阿嬤的『還行』後面通常還有三十分鐘的詳細點評」

en.content:
Took Mom to a Michelin restaurant for Mother's Day.
After the meal she said: "Not bad."
Back home I asked: "Really, just not bad?"
Mom: "Salt was off, heat was wrong, plating was nice but the rice portion was tiny."
Me: "Then why did you say not bad?"
Mom: "My kid paid for it. I had to give face."

That's when I learned —
a mom's 'not bad'
is the most expensive politeness in the world.
en.editorNote: Post-Mother's-Day story caption with a restaurant photo — relatable scene
variations: Add: "Next year I'll just bring her home to her own cooking — she'll actually compliment that."
Grandma version: "Grandma's 'not bad' usually comes with a thirty-minute detailed review."

items.sourceNote: 2026 母親節 王品 龍蝦 優惠 排隊 — 2026-05-10 當日應景

zh-tw.content:
母親節當天的餐廳生態：
上午十一點 — 滿了。
中午十二點 — 滿了。
下午兩點 — 滿了。
晚上九點 — 滿了。

服務生：「請問幾位？」
我：「我們六個人。」
服務生：「不好意思，今天只剩十點半的位置。」
我：「那我們吃宵夜當母親節晚餐。」

媽：「沒關係，我已經習慣了 —
當媽以後，連肚子餓都要排隊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節當天 IG 限動配餐廳排隊照
variations: 也可以接：「我媽說，這是她一年裡最像 VIP 的一天 — 因為要等位」
對外帶版本：「最後我們點龍蝦外帶 — 媽說『這樣也好，不用化妝』

en.content:
The Mother's Day restaurant ecosystem:
11 a.m. — fully booked.
Noon — fully booked.
2 p.m. — fully booked.
9 p.m. — fully booked.

Server: "How many in your party?"
Me: "Six."
Server: "Sorry, only 10:30 p.m. is open."
Me: "Late-night supper as Mother's Day dinner it is."

Mom: "It's fine, I'm used to it —
once you become a mom, even hunger has to wait in line."
en.editorNote: Mother's Day caption paired with a packed restaurant photo
variations: Add: "Mom says this is the most VIP she feels all year — because she has to wait."
Takeout version: "We ended up grabbing lobster to go. Mom said, 'works for me, no makeup needed.'"

items.sourceNote: 2026 母親節 NG禮物 體重計 反向應景 — 食物哲學 cross

zh-tw.content:
母親節送禮三大地雷：
一、體重計。
二、吸塵器。
三、康乃馨配「妳辛苦了」貼紙。

那送什麼最安全？

答案是 —
帶她出去吃飯，
而且要選她吃飽以後
還會說「下次自己煮就好」的那種餐廳。

因為那句話裡 —
才是真的開心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節當天送禮反思限動，可搭配蛋糕或餐桌照
variations: 也可以接：「最高境界是回家吃她煮的 — 她會邊嫌料貴邊偷偷多夾一塊給你」
對小孩版本：「我媽收到體重計那天，全家氣氛比過年掃墓還沉默」

en.content:
Top three Mother's Day gift landmines:
1. A bathroom scale.
2. A vacuum cleaner.
3. Carnations with a "You've worked so hard" sticker.

Then what's actually safe?

The answer:
take her out to eat —
the kind of place where, halfway through dessert,
she says "next time I'll just cook at home."

Because that sentence
is where the real happiness lives.
en.editorNote: Mother's Day gift reflection caption — pair with a cake or dinner table photo
variations: Add: "The peak version is going home for her cooking — she'll grumble about ingredient prices and sneak you an extra piece anyway."
Kid version: "The day my mom unwrapped a scale, the family atmosphere went quieter than tomb-sweeping day."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名 飲料店 — 2026 應景延伸

zh-tw.content:
經過一間飲料店叫「都可以」。
朋友：「為什麼叫都可以？」
我：「因為客人問什麼都可以，老闆就說 — 都可以。」
朋友：「那要點哪一杯？」
我：「都可以。」
朋友：「我認真在問你欸。」
我：「老闆也是認真在回你。」

後來我們在門口站了十分鐘，
證明了一件事 —
選擇障礙，是被店名養大的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 搭配飲料店招牌照限動，自嘲選擇障礙
variations: 也可以接：「最後我們點了招牌 — 因為招牌不會反問你」
對情侶版本：「她問我吃什麼，我說都可以；下一秒我們就開始冷戰」

en.content:
Walked past a drink shop literally called "Anything Works."
Friend: "Why's it called that?"
Me: "Because whatever the customer asks, the owner just says — anything works."
Friend: "So which one should we order?"
Me: "Anything works."
Friend: "I'm seriously asking."
Me: "He's seriously answering."

We stood outside for ten minutes,
which proved one thing —
decision paralysis is raised by shop names.
en.editorNote: Pair with a drink shop signboard photo — self-deprecating take on indecision
variations: Add: "We ended up getting the house special — because the menu doesn't ask follow-up questions."
Couple version: "She asked what I wanted to eat. I said anything works. Cold war started in the next breath."

items.sourceNote: 母親節 媽媽煮飯 廚房 親子料理 — 2026 應景延伸

zh-tw.content:
母親節我說要下廚給媽吃。
進廚房不到三分鐘 —
媽：「鍋拿錯了。」
五分鐘 —
媽：「火太大。」
七分鐘 —
媽：「讓開，我來。」

最後她煮給我吃。
我問：「不是要我煮給妳嗎？」
媽：「你的孝心我收到了，剩下的留給冰箱接住。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節下廚翻車限動配廚房照，超有畫面感
variations: 也可以接：「我媽的廚房只有兩種狀態 — 她在煮，跟她準備接手」
對爸爸版本：「我爸進廚房十年了 — 紀錄是煮一次泡麵就被請出去」

en.content:
Said I'd cook for Mom on Mother's Day.
Three minutes into the kitchen —
Mom: "Wrong pan."
Five minutes —
Mom: "Heat's too high."
Seven minutes —
Mom: "Move, I'll do it."

In the end, she cooked for me.
I asked: "Wasn't I supposed to cook for you?"
Mom: "I got the thought. The rest can be caught by the fridge."
en.editorNote: Mother's Day kitchen disaster caption with a stovetop photo
variations: Add: "My mom's kitchen has two states — her cooking, or her about to take over."
Dad version: "My dad's been entering the kitchen for ten years. Personal best: one cup of instant noodles before getting kicked out."

items.sourceNote: 我再 ven 一次 Threads 早餐店迷因 — 2026 ven 諧音延伸

zh-tw.content:
早餐店阿姨：「妹妹點什麼？」
我：「蛋餅一份。」
阿姨：「飲料呢？」
我：「我再 ven 一下。」
阿姨：「妹妹這裡沒有 ven，只有冰紅茶、奶茶、豆漿。」
我：「那我 ven 一下豆漿。」
阿姨：「妹妹妳是不是上網太多。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 搭配蛋餅或奶茶限動，超應景跟上 2026 早餐店迷因
variations: 也可以接：「她沒罵我，只是默默把豆漿溫熱端到我面前」
對上班族版本：「我每天 ven 的不是飲料 — 是我還想不想去公司」

en.content:
Breakfast shop auntie: "What'll it be, sweetie?"
Me: "One egg pancake."
Auntie: "And to drink?"
Me: "Let me think for a sec."
Auntie: "Sweetie, there's no 'let me think' here. Only iced tea, milk tea, or soy milk."
Me: "Okay, let me think... soy milk."
Auntie: "Sweetie, you've been online way too much."
en.editorNote: Pair with an egg pancake or milk tea story — riffs on the 2026 breakfast shop meme
variations: Add: "She didn't scold me. She just quietly warmed the soy milk and slid it over."
Office worker version: "What I'm really deliberating every morning isn't the drink — it's whether I still want to go to work."

items.sourceNote: 2026 隨餐飲品 趨勢 飲料 佔比 — 上班族厭世延伸

zh-tw.content:
為什麼 2026 大家吃飯一定要配一杯飲料？

因為餐點是給胃的，
飲料是給靈魂的。

沒有飲料的午餐 —
只是「進食」，
不是「吃飯」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 午休限動配便當加手搖照，吐槽自己離不開飲料
variations: 也可以接：「沒喝飲料的那天，我會覺得整個下午都缺一個東西」
對減糖版本：「我說好要戒糖 — 但戒糖是給體重的，不是給靈魂的」

en.content:
Why does every meal in 2026 need a drink on the side?

The food feeds your stomach.
The drink feeds your soul.

A lunch without a drink
is just "eating" —
not a "meal."
en.editorNote: Lunch break caption with a bento-plus-boba photo — own the dependency
variations: Add: "On days without a drink, the whole afternoon feels like it's missing something."
Sugar-cutting version: "I said I'd quit sugar — but quitting is for the waistline, not the soul."

items.sourceNote: 2026 夏天 草莓季 手搖 限定 — 排隊厭世延伸

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「為什麼草莓季的飲料這麼貴？」
我：「因為一年只開放這幾個月。」
朋友：「那為什麼要排這麼久？」
我：「因為我們也只開放這幾個月還有心情排隊。」
朋友：「那喝完呢？」
我：「喝完發現 — 真正季節限定的，是我的耐心。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 草莓季手搖飲限動配排隊照，自嘲自己跟季節一樣短
variations: 也可以接：「我跟草莓一樣 — 都是被高溫淘汰的那種」
對單身版本：「草莓奶蓋一年一次，戀愛十年一次 — 我選奶蓋」

en.content:
Friend: "Why are strawberry season drinks so expensive?"
Me: "Because they're only here a few months."
Friend: "So why are we waiting this long?"
Me: "Because we're also only in the mood to wait for a few months a year."
Friend: "And after you drink it?"
Me: "After, you realize — the real seasonal limited item was my patience."
en.editorNote: Strawberry-season drink caption with a queue photo — joke about your own shelf life
variations: Add: "I'm like a strawberry — gets eliminated the moment the temperature rises."
Single version: "Strawberry cream caps come once a year. Love comes once a decade. I'll take the cream cap."

items.sourceNote: 2026 全台夜市 爆漿脆皮魷魚 IG 暴紅 — 排隊文化延伸

zh-tw.content:
夜市新爆紅小吃的命運：

第一週：IG 限動全是它。
第二週：在地人也願意去排。
第三週：開始有人拍「排到的瞬間」。
第四週：旁邊開了三家一模一樣的。
第五週：排隊的人變成是去拍那三家在打仗。

台灣夜市的爆紅 —
本質上是一種短期就業機會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末逛夜市限動配排隊照，自嘲爆紅文化
variations: 也可以接：「等你終於排到 — 隔壁已經出新口味了」
對在地版本：「在地人都等第六週才去 — 那時候排隊的人已經換成下一家的了」

en.content:
The life cycle of a viral night market snack:

Week 1: every IG story is about it.
Week 2: even locals are willing to queue.
Week 3: people start filming "the moment I got mine."
Week 4: three identical stalls open right next door.
Week 5: the queue is now people filming the three stalls go to war.

Going viral at a Taiwan night market
is basically just a short-term employment program.
en.editorNote: Weekend night market caption with a queue photo — mock the hype cycle
variations: Add: "By the time you finally reach the front — the place next door has a new flavor."
Local version: "Locals wait until week six — by then, the line has already moved to the next stall."

items.sourceNote: 2026 諧音梗店名延伸 — 鹹酥雞攤 在地觀察

zh-tw.content:
巷口的鹹酥雞攤改名了，叫「正鹽法酥」。
我問老闆：「為什麼取這個名字？」
他：「客人問我可不可以少鹽，我就回 — 不行，這是正法。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜想吃鹹酥雞時在 IG 限動發，配排隊照超合適
variations: 也可以改成滷味版本：店名「極享滷意」，客人問可不可以不要香菜，老闆回 — 這是滷的意志
對減肥版本：「少鹽是邪法，多炸才是正道」

en.content:
The fried chicken stall on my corner changed its name to "By the Book Bites."
I asked the owner why.
Him: "When customers ask if I can hold back on the salt, I just say — no, this is by the book."
en.editorNote: Late-night fried-chicken caption, pairs well with a queue photo
variations: Braised-snack version: name it "Braised Intent" — customer asks to skip cilantro, owner says, "This is the intent of the braise."
Diet version: "Less salt is heresy. More frying is the truth."

items.sourceNote: 2026 B級美食 豆花 剉冰 解暑 — 季節時事延伸

zh-tw.content:
夏天的台灣有兩種人：

第一種：「太熱了，吃豆花。」
第二種：「太熱了，吃剉冰。」

我屬於第三種 —
「太熱了，先吃豆花，再吃剉冰，然後跟自己說 — 這是補水。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天午後限動配甜品照，自嘲嘴饞最有效
variations: 也可以接：「醫生說要多喝水 — 沒說不能加糖跟煉乳」
對省錢版本：「冷氣電費太貴，所以我選擇從體內降溫」

en.content:
There are two kinds of people in Taiwan in summer:

Type 1: "Too hot — let's get douhua."
Type 2: "Too hot — let's get shaved ice."

I'm Type 3 —
"Too hot, so I'll get douhua first, then shaved ice, and then tell myself — this is hydration."
en.editorNote: Summer-afternoon caption with a dessert photo — own the sweet tooth
variations: Add: "The doctor said drink more water — they didn't say no sugar or condensed milk."
Budget version: "AC is too expensive, so I cool down from the inside."

items.sourceNote: 2026 逢甲夜市 起司馬鈴薯 黑糖珍奶 創新小吃 — 業配感觀察

zh-tw.content:
朋友從逢甲夜市回來，興奮地說：
「我發現一款新的黑糖珍奶，超讚！」
我：「跟去年那款差在哪？」
他：「多了金箔。」
我：「然後呢？」
他：「然後一杯多三十塊。」
我：「所以本質上 —」
他：「我喝了一口三十塊的金。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 逢甲夜市打卡限動配飲料照，自嘲為了 IG 多花的錢
variations: 也可以接：「金箔在胃裡也不會升值 — 但會在限動裡」
對省錢版本：「我寧可把那三十塊留著加珍珠」

en.content:
My friend came back from Fengjia Night Market all hyped:
"Found a new brown sugar bubble tea — incredible."
Me: "How's it different from last year's?"
Him: "It has gold flakes."
Me: "And?"
Him: "And it's thirty NTD more."
Me: "So basically —"
Him: "I just drank a thirty-NTD sip of gold."
en.editorNote: Fengjia night-market caption with a drink photo — joke about IG tax
variations: Add: "Gold flakes don't appreciate in your stomach — only on your story."
Budget version: "I'd rather use that thirty NTD for extra pearls."

items.sourceNote: 2026 CNN 推薦 臭豆腐 在地飲食文化 — 朋友帶外國人觀察

zh-tw.content:
我帶外國朋友去夜市吃臭豆腐。
他：「為什麼叫臭豆腐？」
我：「因為它聞起來臭，吃起來香。」
他吃了一口，沉默。
我：「怎麼樣？」
他：「我重新理解了 — 原來『香』在台灣是一個比較級。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 帶外國朋友吃夜市限動配臭豆腐照，文化梗最有梗
variations: 也可以接：「跟『辣』一樣 — 都是要在台灣重新校正的單位」
對在地版本：「真正的臭豆腐控 — 是聞到味道就餓了的那種」

en.content:
I took my foreign friend to a night market for stinky tofu.
Him: "Why is it called stinky tofu?"
Me: "Smells stinky, tastes fragrant."
He takes a bite. Silence.
Me: "Well?"
Him: "I now understand — 'fragrant' in Taiwan is a comparative."
en.editorNote: Night market caption with a stinky tofu photo, perfect when hosting foreign friends
variations: Add: "Same goes for 'spicy' — both are units that need to be recalibrated in Taiwan."
Local version: "A true stinky tofu fan — the smell alone makes you hungry."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名 蚵男 — 2026 重新觀察改寫

zh-tw.content:
我朋友開了間蚵仔煎，店名叫「蚵男」。
我：「這名字會不會太自貶？」
他：「不會啊 — 客人吃完才會發現，他自己才是。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搭配蚵仔煎打卡照，自嘲被女友拋下單吃的橋段
variations: 也可以接：「招牌寫『一個人也歡迎』 — 翻譯：客群定位精準」
對情侶版本：「兩個人來吃才不會被店名笑」

en.content:
My friend opened an oyster omelet stand called "Oyster Bro."
Me: "Isn't that name kind of self-deprecating?"
Him: "Nope — customers only realize after eating that they're the bro."
en.editorNote: Pair with an oyster omelet photo, perfect when eating solo on a Friday night
variations: Add: "The sign says 'solo diners welcome' — translation: target demographic locked in."
Couple version: "Come as a pair so the store name doesn't roast you."

items.sourceNote: 丸子 諧音梗 出來丸 — 改寫成對話格式

zh-tw.content:
弟弟問我：「什麼食物最喜歡出門？」
我：「不知道。」
他：「丸子 — 因為牠出來丸（玩）。」
我：「那什麼食物最喜歡待在家？」
他想了三秒：「滷蛋 — 因為牠很宅（窄）。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 親子或長輩 LINE 群組分享首選，老派諧音梗永遠安全
variations: 可接續：「那什麼食物最會考試？— 麻糬，因為牠很黏（撚）題」
上班族版本：「我最喜歡的食物是貢丸 — 因為下班只想攤著被貢」

en.content:
My little brother asked: "What food loves going out the most?"
Me: "No idea."
Him: "Meatballs — they're always rolling out."
Me: "What food loves staying home?"
He thinks for three seconds: "Bread loaves — they just loaf around."
en.editorNote: Family group chat ammo, classic pun energy that always lands with parents
variations: Follow-up: "What food is best at exams? — Mochi, it always sticks to the answer."
Office version: "My favorite food is meatballs — because after work I just want to be rolled around."

items.sourceNote: Lettuce romaine calm 西式生菜諧音 — 重寫成中英對照雙場景

zh-tw.content:
我宣布要減肥，每天只吃生菜沙拉。
第一天朋友問我感覺如何。
我：「冷靜（romaine）。」
第三天他再問。
我：「我跟生菜的關係已經發展到 — 我們互相討厭，但還是天天見面。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合減肥週記限動，配一碗看起來很委屈的沙拉照
variations: 也可以接：「第七天我跟生菜離婚了 — 它得到雞胸肉的撫養權」
外食版本：「便利商店沙拉 — 法律上是食物，情感上是處罰」

en.content:
I announced I was going on a diet — just salad every day.
Day one, friend asks how I feel.
Me: "Lettuce romaine calm."
Day three, he asks again.
Me: "Me and lettuce have reached the stage where — we hate each other, but still meet daily."
en.editorNote: Diet-week story caption with a sad-looking salad photo
variations: Add: "Day seven we divorced — the chicken breast got custody."
Convenience-store version: "Pre-packed salad — legally food, emotionally punishment."

items.sourceNote: pizza one in a melon 西式 食物雙關 — 改寫成餐廳場景

zh-tw.content:
女友：「你覺得我在你心裡是什麼？」
我：「妳是我心中那塊披薩。」
她：「好甜喔！為什麼？」
我：「因為 — 切八塊我也想自己吃完不分人。」
她沉默了三秒：「這算告白還是警告？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 情侶限動梗，搭配披薩約會照最有效，但請確認對方笑點再用
variations: 可接續：「但今天可以分妳一塊 — 因為妳買單」
升級版本：「妳是我心中的鳳梨披薩 — 我知道這很冒險，但我願意」

en.content:
Girlfriend: "What am I to you, really?"
Me: "You're the pizza of my heart."
Her: "Aww — why?"
Me: "Because — even sliced into eight pieces, I still want to eat the whole thing alone."
She pauses for three seconds: "Is this a confession or a warning?"
en.editorNote: Couple-caption gold with a pizza-date photo — read the room before deploying
variations: Add: "But today you can have a slice — because you're paying."
Upgrade: "You're the pineapple pizza of my heart — I know it's risky, but I'm in."

items.sourceNote: 2026 端午連假 6/19 公司禮盒 上班族 — 倒數應景延伸

zh-tw.content:
端午節還有一個多月，公司就先發粽子禮盒了。
同事：「為什麼提早這麼多？」
我：「因為老闆怕我們連假請太久 — 提早送你粽子，等於先把你的心包起來。」
同事：「然後呢？」
我：「然後你就只剩三天 — 因為心已經被綁住了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 收到公司端午禮盒當天 IG 限動配粽子照，吐槽連假請假心情
variations: 也可以接：「老闆送的不是粽子，是提前包好的責任」
對請假版本：「請四休九的人，現在都在想怎麼偷偷把粽子吃完不被發現」

en.content:
Still over a month until Dragon Boat Festival, but my company already handed out zongzi gift boxes.
Coworker: "Why so early?"
Me: "Because the boss is scared we'll take too many days off — sending zongzi early is wrapping up your heart in advance."
Coworker: "And then?"
Me: "And then you only take three days — because your heart's already tied down."
en.editorNote: Story caption when the company zongzi box lands on your desk, paired with a gift box photo
variations: Add: "What the boss sent isn't zongzi — it's pre-wrapped responsibility."
Time-off version: "People who took four days off to make it nine are now sneaking zongzi at their desks so HR doesn't notice."

items.sourceNote: herbs arrive on thyme 諧音 — 改寫成中式廚房 香菜場景

zh-tw.content:
媽問我：「為什麼香菜總是最後才放？」
我：「因為它在等戲份。」
媽：「什麼戲份？」
我：「主角通常最後出場 — 而且讓一半的觀眾退場。」
媽沉默兩秒：「你說我做的菜是觀眾退場的等級？」
我：「不是 — 我說香菜是票房毒藥。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末跟媽媽吃飯後 IG 限動配菜色照，香菜派 vs 反香菜派的萬年戰場
variations: 也可以接：「媽說那不要放，我說 — 主角缺席我心情會壞」
對反香菜版本：「香菜是廚房裡唯一不需要劇本的演員 — 它一上場就有人罵」

en.content:
Mom asked: "Why is cilantro always added last?"
Me: "Because it's waiting for its scene."
Mom: "What scene?"
Me: "The lead actor enters last — and clears out half the audience."
Mom pauses for two seconds: "Are you saying my cooking makes people walk out?"
Me: "No — I'm saying cilantro is box office poison."
en.editorNote: Weekend dinner caption with a dish photo — eternal cilantro vs. anti-cilantro debate
variations: Add: "Mom said skip it then. I said — without the lead, my mood is ruined."
Anti-cilantro version: "Cilantro is the only actor in the kitchen that doesn't need a script — it walks in and somebody boos."

items.sourceNote: 自助餐 計價陷阱 上班族 — 2026 original

zh-tw.content:
中午去自助餐。
夾了三樣菜跟一塊雞腿。
阿姨：「一百四。」
我：「等等，這加起來不是只有八十嗎？」
阿姨：「你那塊雞腿是看你心情夾的，我是看我心情算的。」
我：「那如果我心情不好呢？」
阿姨：「那就一百六。」

從那天起 —
我學會了笑著走進自助餐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班族午休限動配自助餐照，自嘲台灣自助餐玄學定價
variations: 也可以接：「自助餐的價格像股票 — 你看不到漲跌的邏輯，只看得到結果」
對省錢版本：「便宜的秘訣只有一個 — 跟阿姨變熟，但這比減肥還難」

en.content:
Lunchtime, went to a buffet-style cafeteria.
Grabbed three dishes and a chicken leg.
Auntie: "That'll be 140."
Me: "Hold on — shouldn't that only be 80?"
Auntie: "You picked the chicken leg by mood. I priced it by mood."
Me: "What if my mood is bad?"
Auntie: "Then it's 160."

From that day on —
I learned to smile when I walked into a cafeteria.
en.editorNote: Lunch break caption with a cafeteria tray photo — own the mystical pricing of Taiwanese self-serve
variations: Add: "Cafeteria prices are like the stock market — no visible logic, only outcomes."
Budget version: "The only secret to cheap is becoming friends with the auntie — and that's harder than losing weight."

items.sourceNote: 大冰奶 都市傳說 早餐店 — 2026 在地觀察

zh-tw.content:
外國朋友第一次點早餐，問我：「大冰奶安全嗎？」
我：「看你跟廁所的距離。」
他：「那如果我家離公司很遠？」
我：「那建議你選熱奶茶。」
他：「為什麼？」
我：「熱的會給你五分鐘的緩衝 — 冰的是隨機事件。」

他點了熱奶茶。
我點了大冰奶。
那天我遲到了二十分鐘 —
但我笑著遲到的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 帶外國朋友吃早餐限動配大冰奶照，自嘲都市傳說最有梗
variations: 也可以接：「大冰奶不是飲料 — 是台灣人的勇氣測驗」
對通勤版本：「早上喝大冰奶的人 — 都是對自己腸胃有絕對自信的勇者」

en.content:
A foreign friend ordered breakfast for the first time and asked: "Is the iced milk tea safe?"
Me: "Depends how far you live from a bathroom."
Him: "What if my office is far from home?"
Me: "Then I'd recommend hot milk tea."
Him: "Why?"
Me: "Hot gives you a five-minute grace period — iced is a random event."

He got the hot one.
I got the large iced.
I was twenty minutes late that day —
but I was smiling the whole time.
en.editorNote: Breakfast caption with an iced milk tea photo — perfect for explaining the local urban legend to foreign friends
variations: Add: "Large iced milk tea isn't a drink — it's a Taiwanese courage test."
Commute version: "People who drink large iced milk tea in the morning are warriors with absolute faith in their own intestines."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名招牌文化參考 — 牛肉麵店「壁虎奴斗」現象

zh-tw.content:
朋友開牛肉麵店，招牌寫「壁虎奴斗」。
我：「為什麼不寫 Beef Noodle？」
他：「因為寫英文沒人會停下來唸三次。」
結果他店裡每天都有一群人站在門口 — 不是排隊，是在解碼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合 IG 發路邊招牌照配文，自帶台灣諧音梗文化
variations: 也可以接：「他說 Google Map 評論一半都在討論店名 — 沒人提麵」
對行銷版本：「比花錢買廣告划算 — 路人自己幫你免費停留三十秒」

en.content:
My friend opened a beef noodle shop. The sign read "Bee-Foo Noo-Doll" in Chinese characters.
Me: "Why not just write 'Beef Noodle'?"
Him: "Because nobody stops to read English three times."
Now there's a crowd outside his shop every day — not a line, just people decoding the sign.
en.editorNote: Caption for a street sign photo on IG, perfect for the pun-name aesthetic
variations: Add: "Half the Google reviews are about the name — nobody mentions the noodles."
Marketing version: "Cheaper than ads — strangers stop for thirty seconds for free."

items.sourceNote: LINE TODAY 諧音哏店名美食專題參考

zh-tw.content:
夜市新開一家夾娃娃機店叫「親家母」。
我：「為什麼？」
老闆：「因為每次客人夾不到就會喊 — 親家母的！」
我笑：「那你不怕被告？」
老闆：「親家母的，他們罵完還是會繼續投錢。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夜市閒聊或夾娃娃機機台前自嘲用，超有台味
variations: 也可以接：「最後一個十元投下去 — 喊的就是真心話了」
對職場版本：「公司 KPI 跟夾娃娃機一樣 — 看得到、夾不到、罵了還是繼續投」

en.content:
A new claw machine arcade opened at the night market. The name was a pun that sounds like a Taiwanese curse word.
Me: "Why that name?"
Owner: "Because every time a customer misses, they yell the curse — which is also the shop name."
Me: "Aren't you worried about complaints?"
Owner: "Nah. They curse, then they put another coin in."
en.editorNote: Banter material when standing in front of a claw machine after losing five times
variations: Add: "By the last ten-dollar coin, the cursing gets real."
Office version: "Company KPIs are like claw machines — visible, ungrabbable, and you keep coining in anyway."

items.sourceNote: 原創 — 早午餐店價格無奈格式

zh-tw.content:
早午餐店菜單寫「美式拿鐵 180」。
我問店員：「為什麼這麼貴？」
店員：「因為它叫『美式』 — 跟美金一起漲。」
我：「那台式呢？」
店員：「台式更貴 — 因為要進口『台味』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末早午餐結帳被嚇到時 LINE 群組轉發，配收據照超好笑
variations: 也可以接：「我點黑咖啡，店員說『那叫無美式』，加價 30」
對加班版本：「公司茶水間咖啡免費 — 但味道也是免費的等級」

en.content:
A brunch spot's menu listed "Americano — NT$180."
Me: "Why so expensive?"
Server: "Because it's an *Americano* — it rose with the US dollar."
Me: "What about a Taiwanese-style coffee?"
Server: "That's more expensive — we have to import the 'Taiwanese flavor.'"
en.editorNote: Forward to the group chat right after a brunch receipt makes your eyes water
variations: Add: "I ordered black coffee. Server: 'That's a Non-Americano' — and charged me thirty more."
Office version: "Free coffee at work — and the taste is also at the free-coffee level."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自西方食物雙關文化 — 酪梨梗台灣化

zh-tw.content:
我跟酪梨說：「你脂肪那麼高，為什麼大家還說你健康？」
酪梨：「因為我長得貴。」
我：「？」
酪梨：「人類只要花得夠多，就會說服自己很值得。」
zh-tw.editorNote: IG 發超商酪梨吐司照配文，自嘲健康飲食消費心理超有梗
variations: 也可以接：「同理 — 為什麼健身房月費比泡麵貴，大家還是付」
對購物版本：「我不是亂買，我是在投資自己 — 投了三千塊在一罐精華液上」

en.content:
I asked an avocado: "You're packed with fat. Why does everyone call you healthy?"
Avocado: "Because I'm expensive."
Me: "…?"
Avocado: "Humans will convince themselves anything is worth it if it costs enough."
en.editorNote: Caption an avocado toast IG photo with this — self-deprecating wellness humor lands
variations: Add: "Same logic for why gym memberships cost more than instant noodles and we still pay."
Shopping version: "I'm not splurging — I'm investing in myself. Three grand on one serum."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自西方麵包雙關 — 台灣超商化

zh-tw.content:
早上去超商買御飯糰，店員問：「要加熱嗎？」
我：「不用，我自己會冷。」
店員愣了三秒，回我：「先生那是御飯糰，不是你的心。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班通勤 IG 限動配超商照，厭世早晨自嘲一秒到位
variations: 也可以接：「結帳完店員多送我一句『加油』 — 我哭了」
對週一版本：「週一早上的我跟未加熱的御飯糰一樣 — 冷、硬、被人勉強吞下去」

en.content:
Bought an onigiri at the convenience store. The clerk asked: "Heat it up?"
Me: "No need. I'm already cold enough."
The clerk paused three seconds and said: "Sir, I meant the rice ball — not your heart."
en.editorNote: Morning commute IG story with a convenience-store photo — instant Monday mood
variations: Add: "At checkout the clerk said 'hang in there.' I cried a little."
Monday version: "Me on Monday morning is just like an unheated onigiri — cold, hard, and reluctantly swallowed."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自西方鹹酥雞夜市梗 — 台灣化

zh-tw.content:
鹹酥雞老闆問：「要不要辣？」
我：「人生已經夠辣了，雞就算了。」
老闆：「先生，你需要的不是雞，是諮商。」
我：「那加大份的諮商，要切。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週五下班 IG 限動配夜市照超應景，自嘲打工人厭世感一秒到位
variations: 也可以接：「九層塔多一點 — 至少還有人願意陪我」
對失戀版本：「老闆，麻煩給我一份分手套餐 — 雞排切、薯條粉、人生不要」

en.content:
The fried chicken vendor asked: "Spicy?"
Me: "My life is spicy enough. Spare the chicken."
Vendor: "Sir, what you need isn't chicken — it's therapy."
Me: "Make it a large therapy then. And cut it up."
en.editorNote: Friday-night IG story with a night-market photo — peak burnout mood
variations: Add: "Extra basil please — at least something wants to keep me company."
Breakup version: "One heartbreak combo please — chicken cut, fries seasoned, life hold the life."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 seafood diet 英文一行笑話 — 台味版

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我：「你不是在減肥？」
我：「對，我在控制飲食。」
他：「那你手上那包鹹酥雞？」
我：「我控制它不會跑掉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 宵夜時間發限動配鹹酥雞照，減肥失敗系厭世文超有共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「我這叫『策略性進食』 — 策略就是吃」
對運動版本：「我有在運動 — 把食物從盤子運動到嘴巴」

en.content:
Friend: "Aren't you on a diet?"
Me: "Yes, I'm controlling my food."
Friend: "What about that bag of fried chicken?"
Me: "I'm controlling it so it doesn't run away."
en.editorNote: Late-night IG story with a midnight-snack photo — diet-fail relatable energy
variations: Add: "It's called strategic eating — the strategy is eating."
Workout version: "I exercise daily — I move food from the plate to my mouth."

items.sourceNote: 原創 — 手搖飲店員對話格式

zh-tw.content:
手搖飲店員問：「甜度冰塊？」
我：「微糖少冰，人生半糖去冰。」
店員：「先生，菜單上沒有人生。」
我：「對啊，我也找不到。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一上班前 IG 限動配手搖飲照，社畜共鳴度爆表
variations: 也可以接：「那給我一杯『加班特調』 — 全糖去冰，配淚水」
對戀愛版本：「我要的愛情跟手搖一樣 — 微糖、少冰、不要加料」

en.content:
Bubble tea clerk: "Sugar level? Ice?"
Me: "Less sugar, less ice. Half-sweet, no-ice for life too."
Clerk: "Sir, 'life' isn't on the menu."
Me: "Yeah, I can't find it either."
en.editorNote: Monday-morning IG story with a bubble-tea cup — office-drone humor lands
variations: Add: "One overtime special please — full sugar, no ice, served with tears."
Dating version: "I want my love life like my bubble tea — less sugar, less ice, no toppings."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自西方 impasta 雙關 — 台灣早餐店化

zh-tw.content:
蛋餅對著我說：
「我皮薄餡多，捲起來還能切。」
我：「然後呢？」
蛋餅：「你皮厚話多，捲不起來，還老是被切。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 早餐 IG 限動發蛋餅照，自嘲職場被砍預算超有梗
variations: 也可以接：「老闆說我『需要再加點料』 — 我以為他在點蛋餅」
對主管版本：「主管像蘿蔔糕 — 看起來方方正正，咬下去全是粉」

en.content:
An egg crepe says to me:
"I'm thin-skinned, well-stuffed, and easy to roll up and slice."
Me: "And?"
Egg crepe: "You're thick-skinned, full of talk, can't be rolled up — and you keep getting cut."
en.editorNote: Breakfast IG story with a danbing photo — workplace-budget-cut humor
variations: Add: "My boss said I 'need more filling.' I thought he was ordering breakfast."
Manager version: "Bosses are like radish cake — looks neat and square, but it's all starch inside."

items.sourceNote: 飲料封膜笑話格式 — 改寫

zh-tw.content:
封膜上的題目：「為什麼鹹酥雞老闆從來不失戀？」
撕開封膜：「因為他每天都有人來『加辣』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合截圖丟群組，配一杯手搖飲照片更有 fu
variations: 也可以改成：「因為他不缺『回頭客』」
厭世版：「因為他連自己都不愛了，誰來都一樣」

en.content:
Question on the drink lid seal: "Why does the fried chicken stand owner never get heartbroken?"
Peel the seal: "Because someone shows up every day asking for extra spicy."
en.editorNote: Screenshot it, send to the group chat with a bubble tea photo
variations: Alt: "Because the regulars keep coming back."
Emo version: "Because he stopped loving himself first — doesn't matter who shows up."

items.sourceNote: 早餐店冷笑話格式 — 鹹豆漿主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
客人：「老闆，你的鹹豆漿怎麼這麼鹹？」
老闆：「因為我加了三十年的眼淚。」
客人沉默了一秒：「⋯⋯那再來一碗。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上發 IG 限動配鹹豆漿照，社畜共鳴度爆表
variations: 也可以接：「老闆說『加蛋多五塊』 — 我看是加我的命還比較便宜」
短版：「鹹豆漿太鹹？那是老闆的人生濃縮」

en.content:
Customer: "Boss, why is your salty soy milk so salty?"
Owner: "Thirty years of tears, all in there."
Customer pauses: "...One more bowl, please."
en.editorNote: Monday morning IG story with a soy milk photo — peak corporate-burnout energy
variations: Add: "He charges five extra for an egg. My will to live costs less."
Short version: "Too salty? That's just the owner's life, concentrated."

items.sourceNote: 西瓜諧音 melon 雙關 — 台灣化改寫

zh-tw.content:
我跟前任說：「我們吃個西瓜吧。」
他：「為什麼？」
我：「因為我們之間 — 只剩『西瓜』可以聊了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天分手主題梗，發 Threads 配西瓜照超有戲
variations: 也可以改：「我們之間只剩冰塊了 — 連感情都退冰不回來」
對曖昧對象版：「我們什麼都聊，就是不聊我們」

en.content:
I said to my ex: "Let's grab some watermelon."
Him: "Why?"
Me: "Because at this point, the only thing left between us — is watermelon."
en.editorNote: Summer breakup energy. Post on Threads with a watermelon pic
variations: Alt: "Only ice left between us — and even that won't thaw."
Situationship version: "We talk about everything except us."

items.sourceNote: 諧音梗店名 — 滷味店原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友開了間滷味店叫「滷不起」。
我：「為什麼？」
他：「客人嫌貴的時候 — 我就直接念店名給他聽。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 通膨主題梗，丟 LINE 群組抱怨物價超應景
variations: 也可以改成水餃店：「包不起」 — 客人嫌十顆一百貴，老闆指招牌
對房東版本：「我租了間套房叫『住不起』 — 房東說那是反諷」

en.content:
My friend opened a braised-food stall called "Can't Afford to Braise."
Me: "Why?"
Him: "When customers complain it's too expensive — I just point at the sign."
en.editorNote: Inflation-era humor. Drop it in the group chat next time someone gripes about prices
variations: Dumpling shop version: "Can't Afford to Wrap" — customer says ten for a hundred is steep, owner points at the sign
Landlord version: "I rented a studio called 'Can't Afford to Live Here.' Landlord said it's ironic."

items.sourceNote: 海鮮 diet 經典英文梗 — 台灣外送版改寫

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我：「你最近在減肥嗎？」
我：「對啊，我在實行『外送瘦身法』。」
他：「那是什麼？」
我：「就是 — 一打開外送 App，看到價錢，就瘦了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週三半夜餓到打開 Foodpanda 又關掉時發限動，社畜共鳴點滿
variations: 也可以接：「外送費比餐點貴的時候，瘦的不是身體 — 是錢包」
對情侶版：「我跟另一半最近一起減肥 — 一起看著外送 App 默默放下手機」

en.content:
Friend: "Are you on a diet?"
Me: "Yeah, the Delivery App Diet."
Friend: "What's that?"
Me: "You open the app, see the prices — and instantly lose weight."
en.editorNote: Late-night Wednesday IG story when you opened the app and closed it again — pure burnout fuel
variations: Add: "When the delivery fee costs more than the food, it's not your body that shrinks — it's your wallet."
Couple version: "My partner and I are dieting together — we just stare at the app and quietly put our phones down."

items.sourceNote: 早餐店 蛋餅 諧音 — 台味原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
早餐店阿姨問我：「弟弟，今天蛋餅要加什麼？」
我：「加培根、加起司、加蛋。」
阿姨：「不是，我問你 — 蛋餅，要加什麼。」
我：「⋯⋯加油？」
阿姨：「不錯嘛，禮拜一就懂事。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 禮拜一早上發 IG 限動配蛋餅照，配早餐店阿姨的口氣超有畫面
variations: 也可以接：「阿姨還補一句『今天頭髮亂亂的，要記得吃飽』 — 比我媽還像我媽」
對社畜版：「我說『加薪』，阿姨默默幫我加了一片起司」

en.content:
The breakfast shop auntie asked: "What do you want in your egg roll?"
Me: "Bacon, cheese, and an egg."
Auntie: "No, I'm asking — what does the egg roll need."
Me: "...A pep talk?"
Auntie: "Not bad, kid. Monday and already getting it."
en.editorNote: Monday morning IG story with a dan-bing photo — auntie energy hits hard
variations: Add: "Then she said, 'your hair looks rough today, remember to eat enough' — more mom than my mom."
Burnout version: "I said 'a raise.' She quietly added an extra slice of cheese."

items.sourceNote: seafood diet 海鮮梗 — 台灣熱炒版本

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「你最近吃什麼？」
我：「我在吃海鮮餐。」
他：「哇，這麼養生？」
我：「對啊 — 海報上的鮮蝦看一眼，然後吃旁邊的滷肉飯。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週五下班揪熱炒前丟群組，配海產攤照片絕配
variations: 也可以接：「真正的海鮮餐 — 看一下價錢，馬上回家吃泡麵加蝦味先」
對省錢版：「老闆問要不要點魚 — 我說『我只跟魚對眼，不深交』」

en.content:
Friend: "What have you been eating lately?"
Me: "I'm on a seafood diet."
Friend: "Wow, so healthy."
Me: "Yeah — I look at the shrimp on the poster, then eat the braised pork rice next to it."
en.editorNote: Friday after-work group chat bait — pairs perfectly with a stir-fry stall photo
variations: Add: "Real seafood diet — peek at the price, go home, boil noodles with shrimp-flavored chips."
Budget version: "Owner asked if I wanted fish. I said, 'I only make eye contact with it — we're not that close.'"

items.sourceNote: donut worry be happy 經典甜甜圈梗 — 台灣甜點店改寫

zh-tw.content:
前任傳訊：「最近過得好嗎？」
我：「我在吃甜甜圈。」
前任：「為什麼？」
我：「因為它跟我們一樣 —
中間空空的，
外面看起來還挺好的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 分手後一個月發 Threads 配甜甜圈照，文青分手感拉滿
variations: 也可以接：「而且還很甜 — 假的那種」
對工作版：「我的人生跟甜甜圈一樣 — 看起來圓滿，中間其實是洞」

en.content:
Ex texted: "How have you been?"
Me: "Eating a donut."
Ex: "Why?"
Me: "Because it's just like us —
hollow in the middle,
but still looks decent on the outside."
en.editorNote: Post on Threads with a donut photo a month after a breakup — peak melancholy
variations: Add: "And sweet, too — the fake kind."
Work version: "My life is a donut — looks whole, but there's a hole right in the center."

items.sourceNote: 諧音梗店名 — 咖啡廳原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友開了間咖啡廳叫「拿不鐵」。
我：「為什麼？」
他：「客人問拿鐵有沒有買一送一 —
我就指招牌：『拿，不鐵』。」
我：「那如果他堅持呢？」
他：「那我就改念成『拿，不．給．」
zh-tw.editorNote: 通膨時代咖啡漲價主題，丟 LINE 群組吐槽超應景
variations: 也可以改成手搖店：「搖不動」 — 客人問可不可以少糖少冰免費加料
對連鎖咖啡版：「我家附近開了間『美而貴』，主打『美式咖啡，貴得很美』」

en.content:
My friend opened a café called "Latte Not."
Me: "Why?"
Him: "When a customer asks if lattes are buy-one-get-one —
I just point at the sign: 'Latte. Not.'"
Me: "What if they push?"
Him: "Then I read it as 'Latte. Not. For. You.'"
en.editorNote: Coffee-inflation era group chat fuel — drop it next time someone gripes about $7 lattes
variations: Boba shop version: "Shake Not" — customer asks if they can swap ice and sugar levels for free toppings
Chain version: "There's one near me called 'Pretty Pricey' — slogan: Americano so beautiful, so expensive."

items.sourceNote: 煎蛋早餐 厭世格式 — 改寫

zh-tw.content:
早餐店阿姨：「弟弟，蛋要幾分熟？」
我：「跟我對人生的態度一樣。」
阿姨：「所以是？」
我：「破掉的，黏鍋的，回不去的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上排隊買早餐時拿來自嘲，搭配半熟蛋黃流出的照片
variations: 也可以接：「阿姨默默幫我多加一根熱狗 — 她懂」
對加班族版：「跟我老闆給的薪水一樣 — 看起來有蛋黃，戳下去全是水」

en.content:
Breakfast lady: "How do you want your eggs?"
Me: "Same as my attitude toward life."
Her: "Which is?"
Me: "Broken, stuck to the pan, and beyond saving."
en.editorNote: Monday morning self-deprecation in the breakfast line — pair with a runny yolk photo
variations: Add: "She quietly threw in an extra sausage — she gets it."
Salary version: "Just like my paycheck — looks like a yolk, but poke it and it's all water."

items.sourceNote: 夜市 大腸包小腸 擬人格式 — 原創

zh-tw.content:
大腸對小腸說：
「你被我包起來才有人要買。」
小腸冷笑：
「你被我撐著才不會塌。」
旁邊的香菜默默走開 —
它知道沒人想聽它說話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夜市照配發文，諷刺辦公室合作關係超到位
variations: 也可以接：「最後客人付完錢，兩個都被吃掉了 — 平等」
對職場版：「同事跟我就是大腸包小腸 — 互相吐槽，缺一不可，下班各自被生活咬一口」

en.content:
Big sausage says to little sausage:
"Nobody'd buy you if I didn't wrap you up."
Little sausage smirks:
"You'd collapse without me holding you up."
The cilantro on the side quietly walks off —
it knows nobody wants its opinion.
en.editorNote: Pair with a night market photo — perfect office-dynamics jab
variations: Add: "Customer pays, both get eaten anyway — equality."
Workplace version: "My coworker and I are the sausage combo — roasting each other daily, both chewed up by life after clock-out."

items.sourceNote: 咖啡 法庭哏 — 在地化改寫

zh-tw.content:
我跟老闆說：「我要告咖啡機。」
老闆：「為什麼？」
我：「它每天早上把我從『想辭職』騙成『再撐一天』 —
這叫詐欺。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週三下午沒電時發限動，配公司咖啡機照片
variations: 也可以接：「老闆說：『那我也要告你 — 浪費我的咖啡豆』」
對自由業版：「我的咖啡機是共犯 — 它知道我不該再接案，還是默默幫我煮第三杯」

en.content:
I told my boss: "I'm suing the coffee machine."
Boss: "On what grounds?"
Me: "Every morning it tricks me out of quitting and into 'one more day' —
that's fraud."
en.editorNote: Drop on Story Wednesday afternoon when energy crashes — photo of the office machine
variations: Add: "Boss: 'Then I'm suing you — for wasting my beans.'"
Freelancer version: "My coffee machine is an accomplice — it knows I shouldn't take another gig, but it still pours cup three."

items.sourceNote: 吐司 抹醬太薄 分手哏 — 改寫

zh-tw.content:
吐司對奶油說：
「我們分手吧。」
奶油：「為什麼？」
吐司：「你抹得太薄 —
看得出來，你早就在省我了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 感情冷淡期 Threads 發文，配吐司只抹一半的照片
variations: 也可以接：「奶油說：『不是我薄，是你太乾』 — 雙方都有理」
對友情版：「老朋友就像奶油 — 久了會凝固，但抹開還是香的，前提是你願意花力氣」

en.content:
Toast says to butter:
"We need to break up."
Butter: "Why?"
Toast: "You're spread too thin —
I can tell you've been rationing me for a while."
en.editorNote: Cold-phase relationship Threads post — half-buttered toast photo
variations: Add: "Butter: 'I'm not thin, you're just dry.' — both have a point."
Friendship version: "Old friends are like butter — they harden over time, but still spread nice if you put in the work."

items.sourceNote: 鹹酥雞 點餐對話格式 — 原創

zh-tw.content:
鹹酥雞老闆：「要加辣嗎？」
我：「跟我的人生一樣就好。」
老闆：「不辣？」
我：「不，是看起來很辣，吃起來沒味道。」
老闆默默多撒了一把胡椒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週五晚上下班買鹹酥雞配啤酒，丟 IG 限動配紙袋照
variations: 也可以接：「老闆說：『弟弟，胡椒不收你錢 — 算心意』」
對相親版：「我朋友的相親對象就像不辣的鹹酥雞 — 看起來很有料，咬下去才知道是擺盤」

en.content:
Fried chicken stall owner: "Want it spicy?"
Me: "Make it like my life."
Owner: "Mild?"
Me: "No — looks spicy, tastes like nothing."
The owner quietly threw on an extra handful of pepper.
en.editorNote: Friday-night post-work salty snack run — IG Story with the paper bag
variations: Add: "Owner: 'Pepper's on the house, kid — call it sympathy.'"
Blind date version: "My friend's blind date was unseasoned fried chicken — looked loaded, turned out to be just plating."

items.sourceNote: 拉麵 al dente 雙關 — 台味改寫成擬人對話

zh-tw.content:
拉麵對湯說：
「我們在一起這麼久，你也該升溫了。」
湯冷冷回：
「是你自己泡太久 — 才會以為我變淡了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冷掉的關係期 Threads 用，配一碗放到冷掉的拉麵照
variations: 也可以接：「旁邊的滷蛋默默切半 — 它知道沒它調解，這段關係早就糊了」
對工作版：「我跟工作的關係就像拉麵泡太久 — 我以為是熱情冷了，其實是我自己軟掉」

en.content:
Ramen says to the broth:
"We've been together this long — time you warmed up."
Broth, cold:
"You're the one who soaked too long —
that's why I taste bland to you now."
en.editorNote: Cooling-off relationship Threads post — photo of a ramen bowl gone cold
variations: Add: "The soft-boiled egg quietly splits in half — without it mediating, this thing falls apart."
Work version: "My job and I are overcooked ramen — I thought the passion went cold, turns out I just got soggy."

items.sourceNote: 披薩 切片 人生分配 — 西式雙關台味改寫

zh-tw.content:
我問披薩：「你怎麼這麼受歡迎？」
披薩：「因為我願意被切八塊分給大家。」
我：「那你不會痛嗎？」
披薩：「我打工五年了 — 早就麻了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日晚上下週要上班的厭世限動，配披薩外送盒
variations: 也可以接：「我：『所以加班費呢？』披薩：『老闆說算在起司裡了』」
對學生版：「期末小組報告就像披薩 — 八片，但只有兩片真的有料，剩下都是邊」

en.content:
I asked the pizza: "How are you so popular?"
Pizza: "I let myself get cut into eight pieces for everyone."
Me: "Doesn't it hurt?"
Pizza: "Five years in the job —
I stopped feeling it a while back."
en.editorNote: Sunday-night dread Story before Monday — delivery box photo
variations: Add: "Me: 'So where's the overtime pay?' Pizza: 'Boss says it's baked into the cheese.'"
Student version: "A group project is a pizza — eight slices, two have real topping, the rest is crust."

items.sourceNote: 水餃 沸水煮 主管哏 — 原創職場改寫

zh-tw.content:
水餃下鍋前對我說：
「等一下浮起來，不代表我準備好了 —
是水太燙，我只是不想沉下去。」
我看了一眼老闆傳來的訊息 —
默默點了點頭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上開週會前發 Threads，配水餃下鍋的照片
variations: 也可以接：「老闆問：『你今天狀態怎麼樣？』我：『跟剛浮起來的水餃一樣 — 看起來熟了』」
對學生版：「我交報告前的狀態就像水餃 — 浮起來不是完成，是被逼到不能再沉」

en.content:
The dumpling, right before the boil, says to me:
"When I float up later, that doesn't mean I'm ready —
the water's just hot, and I refuse to sink."
I glance at my boss's latest message —
and silently nod.
en.editorNote: Monday-morning pre-standup Threads post — dumplings hitting the pot
variations: Add: "Boss: 'How are you today?' Me: 'Like a dumpling that just surfaced — looks done.'"
Student version: "My state before turning in a paper is a dumpling — floating isn't finished, it's just can't-sink-anymore."

items.sourceNote: 火鍋 群組約飯 朋友消失格式 — 原創

zh-tw.content:
群組丟「要不要吃火鍋」 — 五分鐘內已讀十人。
問「誰要訂位」 — 全員突然下線。
問「AA 制可以嗎」 — 群組瞬間變成密室。
最後是我一個人，對著鴛鴦鍋兩邊都很孤單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末約不到人的 IG 限動文，配一鍋兩湯底的空座位照
variations: 也可以接：「服務生問『一位？』 — 我說『不，是兩種寂寞』」
對家庭版：「家族群組約火鍋 — 長輩說要去，到場剩三個小孩跟我」

en.content:
Group chat: "Hot pot tonight?" — ten read receipts in five minutes.
"Who's booking?" — everyone suddenly offline.
"Can we split the bill evenly?" — the chat turns into an escape room.
In the end it's just me, equally lonely on both sides of the divided pot.
en.editorNote: Weekend nobody-shows-up Story — empty seats around a split-broth pot
variations: Add: "Server: 'Table for one?' Me: 'No — table for two kinds of loneliness.'"
Family version: "Family chat: 'hot pot Sunday?' — elders say yes. Show up: three kids and me."

items.sourceNote: 滷肉飯 便當 撐一天 — 上班族原創

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「中午吃什麼？」
我：「滷肉飯。」
他：「不會膩嗎？每天都吃。」
我：「會啊 —
但只有它願意每天用同一個價錢，假裝跟我一樣沒變。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週三中午便當文 Threads 用，配一碗滷肉飯特寫
variations: 也可以接：「老闆說：『滷汁多給你一匙』 — 全公司只有他懂我」
對自由業版：「我跟滷肉飯的關係就像穩定收入 — 平凡、可預期，且越來越奢侈」

en.content:
Coworker: "What's lunch?"
Me: "Braised pork rice."
Them: "Don't you get sick of it? Every day."
Me: "Yeah —
but it's the only thing willing to charge the same price and pretend nothing's changed, just like me."
en.editorNote: Wednesday lunch Threads post — close-up of a bowl of lu rou fan
variations: Add: "Owner: 'Extra spoonful of sauce on me.' — only person in the company who gets it."
Freelancer version: "My relationship with braised pork rice is steady income — plain, predictable, and increasingly a luxury."

items.sourceNote: 海鮮減肥梗 改寫 — I'm on a seafood diet 經典英文諧音轉中文情境

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「你不是說在減肥？」
我：「對啊，我在吃『看見就吃』減肥法。」
他：「那有效嗎？」
我：「看見什麼吃什麼 —
目前唯一看不見的，是體重計上的數字。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 減肥失敗梗，配一張被外送袋包圍的桌面照超有共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「醫生問我吃了什麼 — 我說『一切看得到的東西』」
對健身版：「我有在運動 — 嘴巴的咀嚼肌每天都很操」

en.content:
Friend: "Aren't you on a diet?"
Me: "Yeah, the see-food diet."
Them: "Is it working?"
Me: "I eat everything I see —
the only thing I can't see anymore is the number on the scale."
en.editorNote: Diet-fail post for a desk surrounded by delivery bags
variations: Add: "Doctor asked what I ate — I said 'everything within line of sight.'"
Gym version: "I do work out — my jaw muscles get a full session daily."

items.sourceNote: 夜市排隊文化 原創 — 2026 台灣小吃熱度延伸

zh-tw.content:
朋友問：「夜市哪攤最好吃？」
我：「最後面那攤。」
他：「為什麼？」
我：「因為我排到時，前面的人已經幫我把難吃的攤都試過了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末夜市文 Threads 限定，配一張人擠人的逢甲或士林照
variations: 也可以接：「我都先看哪攤排隊最長 — 不是因為好吃，是因為觀光客最好騙」
對自助餐版：「自助餐挑菜邏輯一樣 — 看阿姨阿伯夾什麼，跟著夾就對了」

en.content:
Friend: "Which night market stall is the best?"
Me: "The one at the very back."
Them: "Why?"
Me: "Because by the time I get there, everyone ahead of me has already test-tasted the bad stalls for me."
en.editorNote: Weekend night-market Threads post — packed Shilin or Fengjia crowd shot
variations: Add: "I scout the longest line — not for taste, but for spotting tourists who'll try anything."
Cafeteria version: "Same logic at a buffet — watch what the aunties and uncles grab, then copy them."

items.sourceNote: Lettuce turnip the beet 蔬菜諧音 改寫成台味早餐店情境

zh-tw.content:
早餐店點蛋餅，老闆問：「加蔥嗎？」
我：「不要。」
他：「加蛋？」
我：「不要。」
他：「加起司？」
我：「不要。」
他：「那你到底要什麼？」
我：「我要 — 加價以外什麼都不要。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早餐文，配蛋餅特寫 + 一張收據顯示加料總額
variations: 也可以接：「老闆嘆氣：『你這樣我怎麼賺』 — 我說『你不就是想賺我這個嗎』」
對手搖飲版：「點微糖少冰 — 結果一杯比正常糖還貴五塊」

en.content:
At the breakfast shop ordering an egg crepe. Owner: "Scallions?"
Me: "No."
Them: "Extra egg?"
Me: "No."
Them: "Cheese?"
Me: "No."
Them: "Then what DO you want?"
Me: "Anything —
as long as it doesn't come with an upcharge."
en.editorNote: Monday breakfast post — egg crepe close-up plus a receipt of add-on totals
variations: Add: "Owner sighs: 'How am I supposed to make money?' — Me: 'Isn't that exactly what you're trying to do off me?'"
Boba version: "Order half sugar, less ice — somehow costs five bucks more than full sugar."

items.sourceNote: 經典 nacho cheese / impasta 雙關 — 改寫成台灣鹹酥雞點餐情境

zh-tw.content:
鹹酥雞老闆：「弟弟，這份是你的嗎？」
我：「不是我的。」
老闆：「啊不是你點的嗎？」
我：「我說 — 那不是我的，那是『拿叫』的，叫了你就要拿。」
老闆：「拿到帳單也要叫的那種喔？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週五下班買鹹酥雞配啤酒 IG 限動配紙袋照，諧音梗開頭超台
variations: 也可以接：「我朋友開店叫『拿叫食堂』 — 你叫了，就得拿，不然老闆比你還會叫」
對外送版：「外送員打來說『你的拿叫到了』 — 我才知道帳單跟食物是分開送的」

en.content:
Fried chicken vendor: "Kid, is this yours?"
Me: "It's nacho mine."
Vendor: "What do you mean?"
Me: "I mean — once you call it out, you've gotta take it. That's the deal."
Vendor: "The kind where the bill also makes you yell?"
en.editorNote: Friday after-work fried chicken IG Story with the paper bag — classic homophone opener
variations: Add: "My friend opened a stall called Nacho Diner — you call it, you take it, or the owner yells louder than you."
Delivery version: "Delivery guy called: 'Your nacho's here.' That's how I learned the food and the bill arrive separately."

items.sourceNote: pizza my heart 經典雙關 — 改寫成台灣分手厭世情境

zh-tw.content:
前任問：「分手後你怎麼樣？」
我：「我訂了一個八吋披薩。」
前任：「一個人吃得完嗎？」
我：「吃得完 — 因為它至少切八片給我，
你連一片完整的話都沒留給我。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 分手後一週發 Threads 配披薩外送盒，厭世感拉滿但帶幽默
variations: 也可以接：「披薩比你誠實 — 它告訴我幾片就是幾片，不會說『我們再聊』」
對工作版：「我跟披薩一樣 — 被切八塊，每塊都還要笑著遞給別人」

en.content:
Ex asked: "How are you doing since we broke up?"
Me: "I ordered an eight-inch pizza."
Ex: "Can you finish one alone?"
Me: "Yeah — because at least it gives me eight slices.
You couldn't even leave me one whole sentence."
en.editorNote: Post-breakup Threads post with a delivery box — melancholy with a wink
variations: Add: "Pizza's more honest than you — it tells me exactly how many slices, never says 'let's talk later.'"
Work version: "I'm a pizza — cut into eight, and I still have to smile while handing each slice away."

items.sourceNote: espresso yourself 咖啡雙關 — 改寫成台灣上班族厭世情境

zh-tw.content:
心理師：「你最近有好好表達自己嗎？」
我：「有啊，每天早上一杯濃縮。」
心理師：「我說的是『表達』，不是『義式濃縮』。」
我：「我知道 — 但這是我這禮拜唯一濃縮過的東西。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週三下午精神耗盡時發 IG 限動，配 espresso 小杯特寫
variations: 也可以接：「心理師說：『那是逃避』 — 我說：『不，是濃縮版的人生』」
對社畜版：「我老闆叫我表達想法 — 我端了一杯濃縮給他，他說太苦，我說『沒錯』」

en.content:
Therapist: "Have you been expressing yourself lately?"
Me: "Every morning. One espresso."
Therapist: "I meant expression, not espresso."
Me: "I know —
but it's the only thing I've concentrated this week."
en.editorNote: Wednesday afternoon burnout IG Story with an espresso shot close-up
variations: Add: "Therapist: 'That's avoidance.' Me: 'No, it's a concentrated life.'"
Office version: "Boss asked me to share my thoughts — I handed him an espresso. He said it was too bitter. I said, 'exactly.'"

items.sourceNote: 台南諧音店名文化 — 吾燕煮 / 澎漁宴 延伸原創

zh-tw.content:
台南朋友帶我吃飯，路上經過三家店：
「吾燕煮」、「澎漁宴」、「米鹿鹿」。
我：「你們台南人取店名是用抽籤的嗎？」
他：「不是。」
我：「那是？」
他：「是把全台灣的耐心都用光的，這樣店名才會這麼吵。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 南下旅遊在台南夜市拍店招照 IG 限動配文超對味
variations: 也可以接：「招牌一念出口就餓了 — 這就是台南人的設計哲學」
對北部版：「我們北部店名都叫『某某之家』、『某某小館』 — 安靜得像在開讀書會」

en.content:
A Tainan friend took me out to eat. We walked past three shops:
"Wuyan-zhu," "Pengyu-yan," "Milulu" — all puns you have to say out loud to get.
Me: "Do you guys name shops by drawing lots?"
Him: "No."
Me: "Then how?"
Him: "We used up all of Taiwan's patience naming them. That's why the signs are so loud."
en.editorNote: Story caption for a Tainan night market sign photo — pun shop name culture nailed
variations: Add: "Say the name out loud and you're already hungry — that's Tainan design philosophy."
Northern version: "Up north our shops are called 'So-and-so's House' or 'So-and-so's Bistro' — quiet enough to sound like book clubs."

items.sourceNote: 2026 端午節 粽 諧音 ㄓㄨㄥˋ 提前預備 6/19 應景

zh-tw.content:
老闆端午節發紅包，附一張小卡：「祝你工作百發百粽。」
我回：「我比較希望是『粽於下班』。」
老闆：「那要看你今年有沒有『粽生大事』。」
我：「有 — 我『粽於』想離職了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午連假前一週 LINE 群組吐槽老闆專用，搭配粽子貼圖效果加倍
variations: 也可以接：「老闆默默把紅包收回去，說『那你連粽子都別領了』」
對同事版：「我們部門今年端午沒粽吃 — 因為大家都『粽於』離職了」

en.content:
On Dragon Boat Festival my boss handed out red envelopes with a card: "Wishing you a hundred wins."
Me: "I'd rather one win — clocking out on time."
Boss: "That depends on whether you've got any big wins this year."
Me: "I do — I finally won the courage to quit."
en.editorNote: Group chat fuel the week before Dragon Boat Festival — pair with a zongzi sticker
variations: Add: "Boss quietly took the envelope back: 'Then no zongzi for you either.'"
Coworker version: "Our team has no zongzi this year — everyone already won the resignation lottery."

items.sourceNote: Donut underestimate me 經典英文諧音 改寫成台灣甜甜圈早餐情境

zh-tw.content:
早餐店阿姨：「妹妹今天不點蛋餅？」
我：「不要，我要兩個甜甜圈。」
阿姨：「這樣會餓欸。」
我：「不會 — 我這人就是『甜甜』看起來軟，咬下去全是洞。」
阿姨：「妹妹你心裡是不是有事？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上厭世限動配甜甜圈照，諧音梗 + 自我吐槽
variations: 也可以接：「我的人生跟甜甜圈一樣 — 重點都在那個洞」
對減肥版：「醫生說我糖太高 — 我說『我的人格糖分本來就高』」

en.content:
Breakfast auntie: "No egg crepe today, sweetie?"
Me: "No, two donuts please."
Auntie: "That won't fill you up."
Me: "It will — donut underestimate me. I look soft on the outside, but I'm hollow on the inside."
Auntie: "Sweetie, is something wrong?"
en.editorNote: Monday morning burnout caption with a donut photo — pun and self-roast combo
variations: Add: "My life is like a donut — the most important part is the hole in the middle."
Diet version: "Doctor said my sugar's too high. I said, 'so is my personality.'"

items.sourceNote: Olive you / lettuce turnip the beet 蔬果經典雙關 — 改寫台灣婆媽市場情境

zh-tw.content:
傳統市場買菜，老闆娘：「妹妹挑那麼仔細幹嘛？」
我：「我在挑最新鮮的。」
老闆娘：「妹妹啊 — 妳挑老公也這樣嗎？」
我：「對，我都選最青的。」
老闆娘：「難怪到現在還沒結婚 — 青菜是越青越好，老公是越熟越甜。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週末市場買菜限動配青菜照，婆媽金句梗超好笑
variations: 也可以接：「我從此買菜都閉著眼睛 — 怕又被老闆娘點醒人生」
對單身版：「我跟青菜一樣 — 太挑剔，最後都被放到下午三點」

en.content:
At the traditional market, the auntie asked: "Why are you picking so carefully?"
Me: "I want the freshest one."
Auntie: "Sweetie — do you pick husbands the same way?"
Me: "Yeah, I always go for the greenest one."
Auntie: "No wonder you're still single — greens get better the fresher they are. Husbands get better the longer they ripen."
en.editorNote: Weekend market caption with a leafy greens photo — auntie wisdom gold
variations: Add: "Now I shop with my eyes closed — terrified of more life lessons from the produce lady."
Single version: "I'm like a leafy green — too picky, and eventually nobody picks me by three p.m."

items.sourceNote: I'm soy into you / soy sauce 雙關 — 改寫台灣滷味攤情境

zh-tw.content:
深夜滷味攤，朋友問我：「你最近有對象嗎？」
我：「有啊，每天都見面。」
朋友：「真的？是誰？」
我（指鍋子）：「醬油 — 它每天都對我深情投入，把我泡得鹹味滿滿。」
朋友：「你不是在找對象，你是在找鈉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜滷味限動配豆乾照，自嘲單身 + 諧音梗
variations: 也可以接：「我跟滷蛋一樣 — 越泡越入味，但是越泡越孤單」
對加班版：「我跟豆乾一樣 — 被生活泡到變色，還要被人嫌不夠味」

en.content:
Late-night braised snack stall. Friend: "You seeing anyone lately?"
Me: "Yeah, every day."
Friend: "Really? Who?"
Me (pointing at the pot): "Soy sauce. It's soy into me — soaks me until I'm full of flavor every night."
Friend: "You're not looking for love. You're looking for sodium."
en.editorNote: Late-night braised tofu IG Story — single life self-roast with a pun
variations: Add: "I'm like a braised egg — the longer I soak, the more flavor, the more alone."
Overtime version: "I'm dried tofu — soaked in reality till I change color, and still called bland."

items.sourceNote: How do you organize a space party / planet 經典 you planet 雙關 — 改寫台灣火鍋分桌情境

zh-tw.content:
公司尾牙吃火鍋。
主管：「這次怎麼分桌？」
同事：「主管那桌、社畜這桌、新人那桌、要離職的這桌。」
主管：「為什麼要分這麼細？」
同事：「因為大家想夾的菜不一樣 — 你夾未來，我們夾今晚就好。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 公司聚餐前一週群組分享，社畜共鳴拉滿
variations: 也可以接：「離職那桌火鍋最旺 — 因為大家都把怒氣丟下去煮」
對部門版：「我們部門那桌的鍋一直冷掉 — 沒人有力氣加湯」

en.content:
Year-end company hot pot dinner.
Manager: "How should we split the tables?"
Coworker: "Manager's table, worker bee table, new hire table, and the about-to-quit table."
Manager: "Why so detailed?"
Coworker: "Different people are reaching for different things — you're scooping up the future, we're just scooping enough to get through tonight."
en.editorNote: Drop in the group chat the week before the company dinner — peak corporate burnout
variations: Add: "The quitters' table boils hottest — everyone tossed in their rage as soup base."
Department version: "Our table's pot keeps going cold — nobody has the energy to add broth."

items.sourceNote: 芒果冰 夏天 療癒 厭世 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天為什麼一定要吃芒果冰？

因為它跟我的人生剛好相反 —
它是堆得越高越值得，
我是越堆越想躺平。
zh-tw.editorNote: 入夏第一碗芒果冰 IG 限動配文，療癒又厭世
variations: 也可以接：「芒果冰融化只要十分鐘，我融化只要看到行事曆」
對減肥版本：「我跟煉乳一樣 — 明知道不該，還是淋上去了」

en.content:
Why is mango shaved ice a summer must?

Because it's the exact opposite of my life —
the higher it's piled, the more it's worth,
while I just want to lie flatter and flatter.
en.editorNote: Caption for the first mango shaved ice of summer — healing yet burnt out
variations: Add: "Mango ice melts in ten minutes. I melt the second I see my calendar."
Diet version: "I'm just like condensed milk — I know I shouldn't, but I poured myself on anyway."

items.sourceNote: 冬瓜茶 古早味 上班族 厭世 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
為什麼我夏天都點冬瓜茶？

因為它熬得夠久、夠甜、夠古早，
喝下去那一刻，
我會以為自己還在阿嬤家放暑假 —
而不是在公司放生自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日午後上班族厭世限動，配古早味冬瓜茶照
variations: 也可以接：「冬瓜茶是我跟童年唯一還連得上線的東西」
對加班版本：「至少這杯甜的，是今天唯一甜的事」

en.content:
Why do I always order winter melon tea in summer?

Because it's simmered long enough, sweet enough, old-school enough
that the moment I sip it,
I think I'm back on summer break at Grandma's —
instead of abandoning myself at the office.
en.editorNote: Summer-afternoon office burnout caption with an old-school winter melon tea photo
variations: Add: "Winter melon tea is the only line I still have open to my childhood."
Overtime version: "At least this drink is sweet — the only sweet thing all day."

items.sourceNote: 端午 粽子 上班族 連假 — 2026 端午應景 原創

zh-tw.content:
端午連假前，同事問：「你要回家吃粽子嗎？」
我：「要。」
同事：「幾顆？」
我：「我媽包幾顆我就吃幾顆。」
同事：「那如果她包很多呢？」
我：「那這個連假，我就不是在放假，是在還債。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午連假前一週群組分享，預告被媽媽的粽子追殺
variations: 也可以接：「我媽的愛論斤算，我的胃論命撐」
對外地遊子版本：「回不了家的，宅配一箱粽子 — 連思念都用冷凍的」

en.content:
Before the Dragon Boat holiday, a coworker asked: "Going home for zongzi?"
Me: "Yep."
Coworker: "How many?"
Me: "However many my mom wraps, that's how many I eat."
Coworker: "And if she wraps a ton?"
Me: "Then this holiday isn't a break — it's debt repayment."
en.editorNote: Share in the group chat the week before Dragon Boat — pre-warn the zongzi onslaught
variations: Add: "Mom's love is measured by the pound; my stomach holds on for dear life."
Far-from-home version: "Can't make it back? Ship a box of zongzi — even the homesickness arrives frozen."

items.sourceNote: one in a melon 西瓜 雙關 — 改寫台灣夏天告白情境

zh-tw.content:
夏天我切了一盤西瓜給暗戀的人。
他：「為什麼是西瓜？」
我：「因為你在我心裡，是『獨一無二』的。」
他：「這跟西瓜有什麼關係？」
我：「西瓜 — 籽多，但我只想要你這一顆。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天告白限動配西瓜照，諧音梗肉麻但好笑
variations: 也可以接：「對方默默把整盤吃完，然後說『朋友，謝謝這顆友情瓜』」
對單身版本：「我切了一整顆西瓜，最後還是一個人吃完 — 籽都吐給寂寞了」

en.content:
In summer I cut a plate of watermelon for my crush.
Them: "Why watermelon?"
Me: "Because to me, you're one in a melon."
Them: "What does that have to do with watermelon?"
Me: "Watermelons have a lot of seeds — but you're the only one I want."
en.editorNote: Summer confession caption with a watermelon photo — cheesy pun, still funny
variations: Add: "They quietly finished the whole plate, then said, 'Thanks, friend, for this melon of friendship.'"
Single version: "I cut a whole watermelon and ate it alone anyway — spat every seed at my loneliness."

items.sourceNote: 愛玉 古早味 夏天 哲學 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
為什麼夏天一碗愛玉檸檬這麼療癒？

因為它軟軟的、沒什麼脾氣、
被檸檬酸成那樣也不抗議。

它教會我一件事 —
人生很多時候，
你只能當那碗被命運加滿檸檬的愛玉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日午後厭世限動配愛玉照，療癒又有點哲學
variations: 也可以接：「至少愛玉被加冰之後，還是清涼的 — 我被加班之後只剩冷掉」
對上班族版本：「我跟愛玉一樣透明 — 老闆看穿我，也看穿我不想加班」

en.content:
Why is a bowl of aiyu jelly with lemon so soothing in summer?

Because it's soft, has no temper,
and doesn't complain no matter how sour the lemon makes it.

It taught me one thing —
a lot of the time in life,
you just have to be the aiyu that fate keeps topping with lemon.
en.editorNote: Summer-afternoon burnout caption with an aiyu jelly photo — soothing and a touch philosophical
variations: Add: "At least aiyu stays cool once you add ice — I just go cold once you add overtime."
Office version: "I'm as transparent as aiyu — the boss sees right through me, and sees I don't want to work late."

items.sourceNote: 端午 粽 諧音 高中 上班族 — 2026 端午連假 6/19-6/21 應景

zh-tw.content:
端午節前，主管在群組發：「祝大家高『粽』。」
我回：「主管，我們只想要高薪。」
主管：「先把專案『粽』於收尾再說。」
我才發現 — 諧音梗到了老闆嘴裡，全部變成催進度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午連假前一週，辦公室群組吐槽用，配粽子貼圖
variations: 也可以接：「主管的『粽』是動詞，我的『粽』是放假」
對學生版本：「老師說祝大家高粽，結果是叫我們別忘了交報告」

en.content:
Before Dragon Boat Festival, my manager posted in the group chat: "Wishing everyone success!" — a pun on the word for zongzi.
I replied: "Boss, we'd settle for a raise."
Manager: "Let's wrap up the project first."
That's when I realized — every pun, in a boss's mouth, turns into a deadline.
en.editorNote: Use the week before the Dragon Boat long weekend — office group chat snark with a zongzi sticker
variations: Add: "The boss's 'wrap up' is a verb. My 'wrap up' is a vacation."
Student version: "Teacher wished us success, then reminded us not to forget the report."

items.sourceNote: 芒果冰 夏天 6-8月盛產 厭世 — 2026 夏季應景

zh-tw.content:
為什麼夏天一定要吃芒果冰？

因為芒果一年只甜這幾個月，
而我一年只快樂這幾口。

冰會化，芒果會過季 —
所以該享受的時候，別省。
zh-tw.editorNote: 六月芒果季限動配芒果冰照，療癒又有點及時行樂
variations: 也可以接：「我跟剉冰一樣 — 看起來很滿，融化得也很快」
對減肥版本：「芒果是水果，所以芒果冰算健康 — 這是我夏天最堅定的信仰」

en.content:
Why is mango shaved ice a summer must?

Mango is only sweet for a few months a year,
and I'm only happy for a few bites a year.

The ice melts, the mango goes out of season —
so when it's time to enjoy it, don't hold back.
en.editorNote: June mango-season caption with a shaved ice photo — soothing, slightly carpe-diem
variations: Add: "I'm like shaved ice — looks like a full pile, melts just as fast."
Diet version: "Mango is fruit, so mango ice is basically healthy — my most ironclad summer belief."

items.sourceNote: 端午 立蛋 冷知識 對話 — 2026 端午應景

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，朋友認真在桌上立蛋。
我：「你立這麼久幹嘛？」
他：「傳說端午正午把蛋立起來，一整年都會好運。」
我：「那你立三十顆是？」
他：「我運氣差到要批發。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節中午限動配立蛋照，自嘲衰運用
variations: 也可以接：「結果蛋立起來了，他人生還是站不穩」
對上班族版本：「我也想立蛋，可是中午那十二點我還在開會」

en.content:
At noon on Dragon Boat Festival, my friend was seriously balancing an egg on the table.
Me: "Why are you at this for so long?"
Him: "Legend says if you stand an egg upright at noon today, you'll have good luck all year."
Me: "So why thirty eggs?"
Him: "My luck is so bad I'm buying it wholesale."
en.editorNote: Dragon Boat Festival noon caption with an egg-balancing photo — self-deprecating about bad luck
variations: Add: "The egg stood up. His life still couldn't."
Office version: "I'd balance an egg too, but at noon I'm still stuck in a meeting."

items.sourceNote: 夜市 蚵仔煎 香腸 夏天 減肥 — 2026 B級美食 原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天立志減肥，朋友約我逛夜市。
我：「我只看不吃。」
走到蚵仔煎攤前，我停了三秒。
朋友：「你說你只看？」
我：「我在看 — 用很近的距離，看它進我嘴裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天減肥破功限動配蚵仔煎照，自嘲意志力
variations: 也可以接：「我的減肥計畫，活不過一條香腸的香味」
對運動版本：「我一邊吃一邊安慰自己，等等走回家就是有氧」

en.content:
I swore to diet this summer, then a friend dragged me to the night market.
Me: "I'm just looking, not eating."
We stopped in front of the oyster omelet stall. I froze for three seconds.
Friend: "Thought you were just looking?"
Me: "I am — from very close range, watching it go into my mouth."
en.editorNote: Summer diet-fail caption with an oyster omelet photo — self-deprecating willpower humor
variations: Add: "My diet plan can't survive the smell of one grilled sausage."
Exercise version: "I ate it while reassuring myself the walk home counts as cardio."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音梗文化 語言嘉年華 飲料店名 — 2026 延伸

zh-tw.content:
經過一間冰店叫「冰友」。
朋友：「為什麼取這名字？」
我：「老闆說 — 來這的都是『冰友』，吃完一碗就是過命的交情。」
朋友：「那吃兩碗呢？」
我：「兩碗就升級成『冰友以上』，要互相幫對方付帳的那種。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天揪朋友吃冰限動配剉冰照，台味諧音店名梗
variations: 也可以接：「結果他真的請我，原來諧音梗也能拿來凹人付錢」
對曖昧版本：「我們是冰友 — 比朋友冰一點，比戀人甜一點」

en.content:
We passed a shaved ice shop called "Bing You" — it sounds exactly like "ice friend," a play on the word for buddy.
Friend: "Why that name?"
Me: "The owner says everyone here is an 'ice friend' — finish one bowl together and you're bonded for life."
Friend: "And two bowls?"
Me: "Two bowls upgrades you to 'more than ice friends' — the kind who pay each other's tab."
en.editorNote: Summer caption for grabbing shaved ice with friends — Taiwan pun-shop-name humor
variations: Add: "He actually treated me — turns out a pun can guilt someone into paying."
Almost-dating version: "We're ice friends — a little cooler than friends, a little sweeter than lovers."

items.sourceNote: 端午 包粽 包中 考生 諧音 — 2026 應景延伸

zh-tw.content:
考生問：「端午為什麼一定要吃粽子？」
我：「因為『包粽』聽起來像『包中』。」
他：「那我多吃幾顆是不是穩了？」
我：「不，那叫『包腫』 — 你會中的是腸胃科。」
