items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名文化 — 專牛餃煎 鑽牛角尖 改寫 original

zh-tw.content:
看到一間賣牛肉水餃煎餃的店叫「專牛餃煎」。
朋友：「為什麼取這個名字？」
我：「念快一點就是『鑽牛角尖』 — 老闆說做餐飲就是要這種精神。」
朋友：「那如果有人嫌不好吃呢？」
我：「那他比老闆更專業，已經鑽到尖端了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 宵夜或聚餐時在群組分享，搭配水餃煎餃照，台味諧音梗必備
variations: 也可以接：「我點餐也是專牛餃煎 — 站在櫃台前想了十分鐘還是選原味」
對工作版本：「我對報表也很『專牛餃煎』 — 一個數字看一整天」

en.content:
Saw a beef dumpling shop with a name that sounds exactly like the phrase for "overthinking every little detail."
Friend: "Why that name?"
Me: "Say it fast and it means 'splitting hairs' — the owner says that's the spirit you need in food."
Friend: "And if someone complains it's not good?"
Me: "Then they're more of a pro than the owner — already split the hair down to the tip."
en.editorNote: Late-night or group dinner material — pair with a dumpling photo, classic Taiwan pun-shop vibe
variations: Add: "I order the same way — stand at the counter overthinking for ten minutes and still pick original flavor."
Work version: "I overthink spreadsheets too — one number can hold me hostage all day."

items.sourceNote: 英文 grape / great 諧音 — 2026 food puns 改寫，土味情話向

zh-tw.content:
她：「你覺得我們在一起會怎樣？」
我：「會很『葡萄』。」
她：「葡萄？什麼意思？」
我：「英文葡萄是 grape，唸起來就是 great — 跟妳在一起的每一天都很棒。」
她：「那如果哪天變酸了呢？」
我：「酸葡萄也能釀成酒 — 我們就一起醉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給曖昧對象的英文諧音土味情話，配水果盤或紅酒照更有氣氛
variations: 也可以接：「而且葡萄都是一串的 — 我不想跟妳分開，一顆都不行」
對句點版本：「她回『我對酒精過敏』，我整串葡萄瞬間只能當水果」

en.content:
Her: "How do you think it'd go if we were together?"
Me: "It'd be grape."
Her: "Grape? What do you mean?"
Me: "Grape sounds like great — every day with you would be amazing."
Her: "And if it turns sour someday?"
Me: "Sour grapes still make wine — we'll just get tipsy together."
en.editorNote: English pun pickup line for your crush — pair with a fruit platter or wine photo
variations: Add: "And grapes come in a bunch — I don't want us apart, not by a single one."
Period-reply version: "She said 'I'm allergic to alcohol,' and my whole bunch of grapes got demoted to fruit."

items.sourceNote: 台灣夏天 剉冰 配料 厭世 — 2026 改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
為什麼夏天一定要吃剉冰？

因為它把所有甜的東西堆在一起，
讓你忘記底下其實只是一堆碎冰。

跟人生有點像 —
上面鋪滿你以為的快樂，
挖到底才發現會融。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天午後厭世限動配剉冰照，療癒中帶一點哲學
variations: 也可以接：「但融了再叫一碗就好 — 這點剉冰比人生佛心」
對戀愛版本：「煉乳是甜頭，碎冰是真相，配料是你以為的未來」

en.content:
Why is shaved ice a summer must?

It piles every sweet thing on top
so you forget there's just crushed ice underneath.

A bit like life —
covered in the happiness you imagined,
and it all melts once you dig to the bottom.
en.editorNote: Summer afternoon burnout caption with a shaved ice photo — comforting with a touch of philosophy
variations: Add: "But when it melts you just order another — shaved ice is kinder than life that way."
Romance version: "The condensed milk is the bait, the crushed ice is the truth, the toppings are the future you imagined."

items.sourceNote: 西瓜 切法 家庭 對話 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
我：「媽，西瓜幫我切小塊一點。」
媽：「自己拿湯匙挖。」
我：「那不就整顆都是我的？」
媽：「對，所以你要負責吃完。」

我這才懂 —
媽不是懶得切，
是用一支湯匙，把責任也一起交給我了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天回家吃西瓜限動配西瓜照，全台灣小孩都被湯匙挖過
variations: 也可以接：「挖到中間最甜，挖到邊邊就是現實」
對手足版本：「一顆西瓜兩支湯匙，從中間開挖就是手足戰爭的起點」

en.content:
Me: "Mom, can you cut the watermelon into small pieces?"
Mom: "Grab a spoon and scoop it yourself."
Me: "But then the whole thing is mine?"
Mom: "Right, so you're finishing it."

That's when I understood —
Mom wasn't too lazy to cut it.
With one spoon, she handed me the responsibility too.
en.editorNote: Summer-at-home watermelon caption — every kid in Taiwan has been handed the spoon
variations: Add: "The middle is the sweetest, the edges are just reality."
Sibling version: "One watermelon, two spoons, both starting from the center — that's where sibling warfare begins."

items.sourceNote: 英文 melon / one in a melon 諧音 — 2026 food puns 改寫，土味情話向

zh-tw.content:
她：「我有那麼特別嗎？」
我：「妳是百萬中選一的『瓜』。」
她：「瓜？你罵我傻瓜？」
我：「英文是 one in a melon，唸起來就是 one in a million — 妳是百萬中的唯一。」
她：「那如果哪天瓜爛了呢？」
我：「爛瓜也是我的瓜 — 我照甜不誤。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給曖昧對象的英文諧音土味情話，配西瓜或水果盤照更應景
variations: 也可以接：「而且西瓜要敲過才知道甜不甜 — 我已經敲定是妳了」
對句點版本：「她回『我比較喜歡哈密瓜』，我整個夏天瞬間沒了滋味」

en.content:
Her: "Am I really that special?"
Me: "You're one in a melon."
Her: "A melon? Are you calling me dumb?"
Me: "One in a melon sounds like one in a million — you're the only one out of a million."
Her: "And if the melon goes bad someday?"
Me: "A bad melon is still my melon — I'd find it sweet anyway."
en.editorNote: English pun pickup line for your crush — pair with a watermelon or fruit platter photo
variations: Add: "And you have to knock a melon to know if it's sweet — I already knocked, and it's you."
Period-reply version: "She said 'I prefer cantaloupe,' and my whole summer lost its flavor."

items.sourceNote: 手搖飲 冰塊 上班族 厭世 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天點手搖的標準流程：
少糖 — 為了健康。
去冰 — 為了多一點飲料。
結果店員說：「去冰會比較少喔。」

我才發現 —
原來連我手裡這杯，
都在教我什麼叫做「想要的越多，得到的越少」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天下班買手搖限動配飲料照，厭世又共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「最後我點正常冰 — 跟人生一樣，妥協最划算」
對減肥版本：「我少糖去冰加珍珠 — 健康跟欲望各退一步，誰也沒贏」

en.content:
Summer drink-ordering protocol:
Less sugar — for health.
No ice — for more drink.
Then the clerk says: "No ice means less in the cup."

That's when it hit me —
even the cup in my hand
was teaching me: the more you want, the less you get.
en.editorNote: Summer after-work bubble tea caption — relatable burnout vibe
variations: Add: "In the end I went with regular ice — like life, compromise is the best deal."
Diet version: "Less sugar, no ice, extra pearls — health and desire each gave ground, nobody won."

items.sourceNote: seafood diet 諧音 厭世上班族 cross — 2026 food puns 改寫

zh-tw.content:
同事：「你最近在減肥？吃什麼？」
我：「海鮮減肥法。」
同事：「哇，那很健康欸。」
我：「英文叫 seafood diet — see food，看到食物就吃。」
同事：「那不是沒在減？」
我：「對，但至少我『看見』自己的問題了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 減肥失敗週末群組吐槽用，英文諧音梗配雞排照
variations: 也可以接：「我這方法唯一的限制 — 就是看不見的食物不吃」
對自律版本：「別人的自律是計算卡路里，我的自律是計算還剩幾口」

en.content:
Coworker: "You're on a diet? What are you eating?"
Me: "The seafood diet."
Coworker: "Wow, that's healthy."
Me: "It's a see-food diet — I see food, I eat it."
Coworker: "So you're not dieting at all?"
Me: "Right, but at least I can finally see my problem."
en.editorNote: Failed-diet weekend group chat fuel — English pun with a fried chicken photo
variations: Add: "The only rule of my diet — if I can't see the food, I don't eat it."
Discipline version: "Other people's discipline counts calories. Mine counts how many bites are left."

items.sourceNote: impasta 假披薩 諧音 — 2026 food puns 改寫成台味

zh-tw.content:
餐廳上錯菜，端來一盤吃起來怪怪的義大利麵。
我：「老闆，這真的是義大利麵嗎？」
老闆：「當然，純正進口。」
我：「可是它吃起來像泡麵。」
老闆：「那就是 — 義大利的『冒牌』貨。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 踩雷餐廳限動配麵照，吐槽觀光區義式料理
variations: 也可以接：「真的義大利人吃到會當場含淚回國」
對自助餐版本：「自助餐的義大利麵，靈魂是台灣的，護照是義大利的」

en.content:
The restaurant brought the wrong dish — a plate of suspicious-looking spaghetti.
Me: "Is this really Italian pasta?"
Owner: "Of course, imported."
Me: "It tastes like instant noodles."
Owner: "Then it's an im-pasta."
en.editorNote: Caption for a tourist-trap pasta photo on stories
variations: Add: "A real Italian would tear up and fly home on the spot."
Buffet version: "Buffet spaghetti — Taiwanese soul, Italian passport."

items.sourceNote: 端午節 粽子 上班族 厭世 — 2026 端午梗圖延伸

zh-tw.content:
為什麼上班族最懂粽子？

因為我們也是 —
被綁得緊緊的，
外表看起來很完整，
內心其實一堆雜七雜八硬塞進去的。

差別是粽子放三天還能蒸來吃，
我放三天就臭了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午連假前最後一天上班，限動配粽子照
variations: 也可以接：「綁我的那條繩子叫 KPI」
對加班版本：「老闆說『辛苦了』的時候，我就被多綁了一圈」

en.content:
Why do office workers understand zongzi best?

Because we're the same —
tied up tight,
looking neat on the outside,
but stuffed with random odds and ends inside.

The difference: a zongzi still steams up fine after three days.
Leave me three days and I go bad.
en.editorNote: Last workday before the Dragon Boat long weekend — pair with a zongzi photo
variations: Add: "The string that ties me up is called KPI."
Overtime version: "Every time the boss says 'good work,' I get wrapped one loop tighter."

items.sourceNote: ketchup catch up 諧音 友情 — 2026 food puns 改寫

zh-tw.content:
好久不見的朋友約吃漢堡。
他：「最近過得怎樣？快跟我說。」
我：「等等，先沾個番茄醬。」
他：「都什麼時候了還沾醬。」
我：「不沾醬怎麼跟你 catch up — 我這叫『沾』近距離。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 久違朋友聚餐限動配漢薯照，玩中英諧音梗
variations: 也可以接：「友情跟薯條一樣 — 要趁熱，涼了就軟掉」
對遠距版本：「我們一年沾一次醬，剛好把一年的事補完」

en.content:
Met up with an old friend over burgers.
Him: "So how've you been? Tell me everything."
Me: "Hold on, let me grab the ketchup."
Him: "Now? Seriously?"
Me: "How else am I supposed to ketchup with you?"
en.editorNote: Reunion meal caption with a burger-and-fries photo
variations: Add: "Friendship is like fries — best while hot, soggy once it cools."
Long-distance version: "We ketchup once a year — just enough to refill the whole year."

items.sourceNote: 夏天 高溫 飲料 融化 厭世 — 2026 夏天迷因延伸

zh-tw.content:
夏天買一杯手搖，走到捷運站。
冰塊化了一半，杯子全是水。

我看著那杯越來越淡的飲料想 —
它跟我的人生一樣，
出門時滿滿期待，
到目的地只剩稀釋過的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日高溫日 IG 限動配融化飲料照，厭世又應景
variations: 也可以接：「全糖出門，到公司只剩微糖的命」
對通勤版本：「我跟冰塊一起在月台融化，差別是它融完還能喝」

en.content:
Bought a bubble tea in summer, walked to the MRT station.
Half the ice melted, the cup turned to water.

Watching it get weaker and weaker, I thought —
it's just like my life:
leave home full of expectations,
arrive a diluted version of yourself.
en.editorNote: Heatwave caption with a melting-drink photo — relatable and seasonal
variations: Add: "Left home full sugar, reached the office with the willpower of light sugar."
Commute version: "The ice and I melt on the platform together — the difference is it's still drinkable after."

items.sourceNote: 2026 馬年 馬倒成功 喪萌迷因 + 手搖飲廢水 — 跨梗延伸

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「你又在喝廢水喔？」
我：「這不是廢水，這是快樂水。」
朋友：「全糖黑糖鮮奶不是廢水什麼是？」
我：「人生已經夠廢了，至少水要快樂的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 下午茶手搖飲限動配文，自嘲又療癒
variations: 也可以接：「我戒得掉甜點，戒不掉那杯安慰我的廢水」
對減肥版本：「廢水的熱量我認了，反正我整個人也快報廢了」

en.content:
Friend: "Drinking your 'junk water' again?"
Me: "It's not junk water, it's happy water."
Friend: "Full-sugar brown sugar milk isn't junk water, then what is?"
Me: "My life's already junk — at least the water gets to be happy."
en.editorNote: Afternoon bubble tea caption — self-deprecating and comforting
variations: Add: "I can quit dessert, but I can't quit the drink that comforts me."
Diet version: "I'll take the calories — I'm basically scrap metal anyway."

items.sourceNote: 夏天 西瓜 擬人 厭世 — 2026 夏季原創

zh-tw.content:
西瓜對著我說：
「我外表硬，內心軟，又紅又甜。
你呢 — 外表軟，內心硬，整天黑著一張臉。」

我切了它。
夏天的勝負，從來不是用講的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日吃西瓜限動配文，無厘頭又消暑
variations: 也可以接：「會頂嘴的水果，下場都很涼」
對上班族版本：「我也想像西瓜一樣紅，可惜我只紅在帳單上」

en.content:
A watermelon said to me:
"Tough on the outside, soft inside, red and sweet.
You? Soft outside, hard inside, and scowling all day."

So I sliced it.
In summer, you don't win arguments by talking.
en.editorNote: Summer watermelon caption — absurd and refreshing
variations: Add: "Fruit that talks back always ends up on ice."
Office version: "I wish I were red like a watermelon — sadly I'm only in the red on my bills."

items.sourceNote: 2026 父親節 餐廳訂位 燒烤 啤酒 家庭 — 8/8 應景

zh-tw.content:
問爸：「父親節想吃哪間？我訂位。」
爸：「隨便，不用花錢。」
我：「那訂燒烤吃到飽喔？」
爸：「好啊。」
我：「不是說不用花錢？」
爸：「我說的是『你的錢不用花』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親節前夕限動配燒烤照，全台灣兒女都懂
variations: 也可以接：「我爸的『隨便』是有附帶條件的隨便」
對啤酒版本：「爸默默又點了一手台啤，說『難得，配肉』」

en.content:
Asked Dad: "Where do you want to eat for Father's Day? I'll book it."
Dad: "Anywhere, don't spend money."
Me: "So all-you-can-eat barbecue, then?"
Dad: "Sure."
Me: "Didn't you say don't spend money?"
Dad: "I said don't spend YOUR money."
en.editorNote: Father's Day eve caption with a barbecue photo — every grown kid relates
variations: Add: "My dad's 'anywhere' comes with terms and conditions."
Beer version: "Dad quietly ordered another round of beer — 'special occasion, goes with the meat.'"

items.sourceNote: 諧音 店名 剉冰 夏天 — 台灣諧音店名文化延伸

zh-tw.content:
經過一間剉冰店叫「冰友」。
朋友：「為什麼叫這個？」
我：「老闆說 — 真正的冰友，會在你最熱的時候出現，融化之前都陪著你。」
朋友：「那融化之後呢？」
我：「那叫塑膠冰友。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日吃冰限動配剉冰照，諧音梗加好友哏
variations: 也可以接：「最怕點完冰，發現它比朋友還快離開我」
對單身版本：「一碗冰至少不會已讀不回」

en.content:
Passed a shaved ice shop called "Bing You" — it sounds exactly like "close friend" in Mandarin.
Friend: "Why that name?"
Me: "The owner says a real friend shows up when you're at your hottest and sticks around till they melt."
Friend: "And after they melt?"
Me: "That's a plastic friend."
en.editorNote: Summer shaved ice caption — pun plus a friendship gag
variations: Add: "My biggest fear: the ice leaves me faster than my friends do."
Single version: "At least a bowl of ice never leaves me on read."

items.sourceNote: 夏天 冷氣 火鍋 矛盾 上班族 厭世 — 2026 夏季原創

zh-tw.content:
為什麼台灣人夏天還要吹著冷氣吃火鍋？

因為我們把電費浪費在製造寒冷，
再把寒冷浪費在製造熱量。

這不是矛盾 —
這是我們對人生唯一的掌控感。
zh-tw.editorNote: 盛夏冷氣房火鍋聚餐限動配文，厭世又好笑
variations: 也可以接：「電費跟火鍋費一起燒，但至少這鍋是我自己選的」
對上班族版本：「上班沒得選，吃火鍋總可以自己決定要不要加辣」

en.content:
Why do Taiwanese people eat hot pot with the AC blasting in summer?

We burn money making it cold,
then burn the cold making it hot.

It's not a contradiction —
it's the only sense of control we have over our lives.
en.editorNote: Midsummer AC-blasting hot pot caption — burnout humor
variations: Add: "The electric bill and the hot pot bill burn together — but at least I chose this pot."
Office version: "Can't choose anything at work, but I can always choose whether to make the broth spicy."

items.sourceNote: 2026 夏季手搖 西瓜烏龍 紅肉西瓜 消暑 — 夏季原創

zh-tw.content:
今年夏天最紅的飲料是西瓜烏龍。
紅肉西瓜配烏龍茶 —
甜的負責讓你開心，
茶的負責讓你清醒。

跟我的人生剛好相反：
甜的早就喝完了，
剩下的全是苦的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 盛夏買手搖時 IG 限動配西瓜烏龍照，厭世又消暑
variations: 也可以接：「喝到最後那口冰塊化掉的水，就是我這個月的存款」
對減肥版本：「我以為西瓜很健康 — 直到看到糖度寫全糖」

en.content:
This summer's hottest drink is watermelon oolong.
Red watermelon plus oolong tea —
the sweet part keeps you happy,
the tea part keeps you awake.

Which is the exact opposite of my life:
the sweet part is long gone,
and all that's left is bitter.
en.editorNote: Midsummer bubble tea caption with a watermelon oolong photo — cooling and a little jaded
variations: Add: "That last sip of melted-ice water is exactly my bank balance this month."
Diet version: "I thought watermelon was healthy — until I saw I ordered full sugar."

items.sourceNote: 士林夜市 芒果冰 排隊 夏天 — 2026 夜市懶人包延伸

zh-tw.content:
夏天排隊買芒果冰的心路歷程：
第十分鐘 — 還好。
第二十分鐘 — 有點後悔。
第三十分鐘 — 我為什麼在這。

輪到我那一刻，
冰山堆滿芒果端到面前 —
剛剛的後悔，瞬間全部融化。

跟那座冰一起。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日夜市排隊買芒果冰時限動配人潮照，自嘲又療癒
variations: 也可以接：「排隊三十分鐘，吃完五分鐘 — 但那五分鐘我願意再排一次」
對戀愛版本：「芒果冰跟暗戀一樣 — 等很久，化很快」

en.content:
The emotional journey of lining up for mango shaved ice in summer:
Minute ten — fine.
Minute twenty — slight regret.
Minute thirty — why am I here.

Then it's my turn,
a mountain of ice piled with mango lands in front of me —
and all that regret melts instantly.

Right along with the ice.
en.editorNote: Summer night market mango ice caption with a queue photo — self-deprecating and soothing
variations: Add: "Thirty minutes in line, five minutes to eat — but I'd line up again for those five."
Crush version: "Mango ice is like a crush — long wait, melts fast."

items.sourceNote: 父親節 餐桌 爸爸 沉默的愛 — 2026-08 應景原創

zh-tw.content:
父親節吃飯，問爸：「最後一塊雞腿你吃還是我吃？」
爸：「我不愛吃雞腿。」

我想了一下 —
從小到大，
他從來沒愛吃過任何一塊好的部位。

原來「我不愛吃」
是爸爸最不會說出口的愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親節前後限動配餐桌照，溫馨向，全台灣孩子都懂
variations: 也可以接：「長大後我才發現，爸不愛吃的東西剛好都是最好吃的」
對阿公版本：「阿公也說他不愛吃魚肚，只愛吃魚頭 — 騙了我二十年」

en.content:
Father's Day dinner, I asked Dad: "You want the last chicken drumstick, or should I?"
Dad: "I don't really like drumsticks."

I thought about it —
my whole life,
he never liked any of the good cuts.

Turns out "I don't like it"
is the love Dad could never say out loud.
en.editorNote: Father's Day caption with a dinner table photo — heartfelt, every kid in Taiwan gets it
variations: Add: "Growing up, I realized the things Dad 'didn't like' were always the tastiest."
Grandpa version: "Grandpa says he only likes the fish head, not the belly — fooled me for twenty years."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名 冰店 夏天 — 大盜陳 文化延伸

zh-tw.content:
經過一間冰店叫「透心涼」。
朋友：「夏天取這名字很聰明欸。」
我：「老闆說 — 吃完冰透心涼，看完帳單也透心涼。」
朋友：「兩種涼不一樣吧？」
我：「一種涼在嘴裡，一種涼在心裡，老闆很公平，兩種都給你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日吃冰限動配剉冰照，諧音梗加上消費吐槽
variations: 也可以接：「最透心涼的是看到只收現金那塊牌子」
對加料版本：「加一堆料 — 涼到心裡，痛到錢包」

en.content:
Passed an ice shop called "Chill to the Bone."
Friend: "Smart name for summer."
Me: "The owner says you chill to the bone eating the ice, and chill to the bone seeing the bill."
Friend: "Those two chills aren't the same, though."
Me: "One chills your mouth, one chills your heart — the owner's fair, he gives you both."
en.editorNote: Summer shaved ice caption with a dessert photo — pun plus a price gag
variations: Add: "The most bone-chilling part is the 'cash only' sign."
Extra toppings version: "Load it up — chills your heart, hurts your wallet."

items.sourceNote: 夜市 雞排 夏天 排汗 厭世 上班族 cross — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天的夜市最矛盾：
汗流到眼睛都睜不開，
手上還是要拿一塊剛炸好的雞排。

一邊流汗，一邊吸熱量 —
身體在減重，意志在增肥。

最後是意志贏了。
它每次都贏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏夜逛夜市買雞排限動配照片，厭世自嘲
variations: 也可以接：「天氣熱到雞排還沒涼，我先涼了」
對減肥版本：「我以為流汗能抵銷熱量 — 直到我又加點了一份」

en.content:
Summer night markets are pure contradiction:
sweat dripping into your eyes,
yet you're still holding a fresh-fried chicken cutlet.

Sweating it out, soaking calories in —
body losing weight, willpower gaining it.

In the end, willpower wins.
It always wins.
en.editorNote: Summer night market fried chicken caption with a photo — burnout self-mockery
variations: Add: "It's so hot the chicken hasn't cooled down, but I already have."
Diet version: "I thought sweating cancels calories — until I ordered a second one."

items.sourceNote: 台南 芒果冰 多品種拼盤 愛文 金煌 厭世 cross — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
台南那碗芒果冰一次放四五種芒果：
愛文的香、金煌的肉、土芒果的酸。

一碗冰裡有三種個性，
還能和平共處。

比我們公司開會強多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天吃芒果冰限動配照，順便吐槽公司
variations: 也可以接：「至少它們吵歸吵，最後都化成同一灘水」
對家庭版本：「我家年夜飯也是這樣 — 各種口味同桌，誰也不讓誰」

en.content:
That bowl of Tainan mango ice packs four or five mango types at once:
the fragrant ones, the meaty ones, the sour little wild ones.

Three personalities in one bowl,
and they still coexist peacefully.

More than I can say for our office meetings.
en.editorNote: Summer mango ice photo caption with a side of office venting
variations: Add: "At least when they argue, they all melt into the same puddle in the end."
Family version: "Our holiday dinner is the same — every flavor at one table, nobody backing down."

items.sourceNote: 雪花冰 綿密 融化 上班族 厭世 — 2026 原創

zh-tw.content:
為什麼夏天的雪花冰要趕快吃？

因為它跟週末一樣 —
看起來蓬鬆又綿密，
一不注意，就化成水了。

而且化掉的部分，永遠最甜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日下午吃冰限動配雪花冰照，配厭世文
variations: 也可以接：「我的休假也是 — 還沒開始享受就剩底下那灘」
對戀愛版本：「熱戀就像雪花冰 — 化得快，但化掉的最甜」

en.content:
Why do you have to eat summer snow ice fast?

Because it's just like the weekend —
looks fluffy and soft,
then you blink and it's a puddle.

And the melted part is always the sweetest.
en.editorNote: Sunday afternoon snow ice photo caption with a burnout note
variations: Add: "My time off is the same — gone before I enjoy it, just the puddle left."
Romance version: "New love is like snow ice — melts fast, but the melted part tastes sweetest."

items.sourceNote: 台灣諧音店名文化 — 冰店 芒果你的 諧音延伸

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「為什麼這家冰店叫『芒果你的』？」
我：「老闆說 — 這碗芒果，是你的。」
朋友：「廢話，我付錢了。」
我：「他的意思是 — 吃完之後，帳單也是你的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天逛街看到諧音冰店時 IG 限動配照
variations: 也可以接：「隔壁那家叫『芒然若失』 — 吃完發現錢包空了」
對情侶版本：「『芒果你的』下一句是『心也是你的』，老闆很會」

en.content:
Friend: "Why is this ice shop called 'Mango Is Yours'?"
Me: "The owner says — this bowl of mango, it's yours."
Friend: "Obviously, I paid for it."
Me: "What he means is — once you finish, the bill is yours too."
en.editorNote: Summer pun-shop ice photo caption for stories
variations: Add: "The shop next door is called 'Mango, So Lost' — finish it and your wallet's empty."
Couple version: "After 'Mango Is Yours' comes 'my heart is yours too.' The owner's smooth."

items.sourceNote: 夜市 巨無霸雞排 夏天 排隊 觀光客 對話 — 2026 trend

zh-tw.content:
觀光客：「為什麼台灣的雞排要做這麼大？」
我：「因為小的不夠拍照。」
觀光客：「所以是為了 IG？」
我：「不，是為了讓你拍完照、排完隊、流完汗之後，
覺得自己沒有白來。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天帶外國朋友逛夜市買巨無霸雞排限動配照
variations: 也可以接：「比臉大的不是雞排，是你的決心」
對減肥版本：「拍照那 30 秒的快樂，要用一週的罪惡感償還」

en.content:
Tourist: "Why is Taiwanese fried chicken made so huge?"
Me: "Because a small one isn't photogenic."
Tourist: "So it's for Instagram?"
Me: "No — it's so that after you take the photo, wait in line, and sweat through your shirt,
you feel like the trip wasn't wasted."
en.editorNote: Summer night market caption when hosting friends with a giant cutlet
variations: Add: "It's not the cutlet that's bigger than your face — it's your determination."
Diet version: "Thirty seconds of photo joy, repaid with a week of guilt."

items.sourceNote: 2026 手搖飲 大洗牌 排名 厭世 上班族 cross — 2026 trend

zh-tw.content:
今年手搖飲排名又大洗牌。
去年第一名，今年掉出前五。

手搖飲的世界跟職場一樣 —
你以為穩坐冠軍，
結果一杯新品上市，
大家就忘了你的存在。

還好我從來不是第一名，
沒有什麼好掉的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到手搖飲排名新聞時群組分享，自嘲上班族
variations: 也可以接：「至少珍奶不會被資遣，我不一定」
對職場版本：「新人就是那杯新品 — 香，但不一定撐得久」

en.content:
This year's bubble tea rankings got shuffled again.
Last year's number one didn't even make the top five.

The bubble tea world is like the office —
you think you're sitting comfortably at the top,
then one new drink launches
and everyone forgets you exist.

Good thing I was never number one.
Nothing to fall from.
en.editorNote: Share in the group chat when bubble tea ranking news drops — office self-mockery
variations: Add: "At least bubble tea never gets laid off. I can't say the same."
Workplace version: "The new hire is that new drink — fragrant, but no guarantee it lasts."

items.sourceNote: impasta / fake noodle 諧音梗改寫

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「你覺得他這個人怎麼樣？」
我：「他就像泡麵 —
看起來很有料，
撕開調味包才發現，
香味全是假的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 辦公室遇到表裡不一的人時群組吐槽用
variations: 也可以接：「而且還很會泡，三分鐘就能膨脹一倍」
對主管版本：「他的承諾跟泡麵圖片一樣 — 僅供參考」

en.content:
Coworker: "What do you think of that guy?"
Me: "He's like instant noodles —
looks loaded with good stuff,
then you open the flavor packet
and realize the aroma was all fake."
en.editorNote: Group-chat shade when dealing with a two-faced colleague
variations: Add: "And he doubles in size in three minutes flat."
Office version: "His promises are like the noodle box photo — serving suggestion only."

items.sourceNote: big dill / pepper a-salt 諧音梗種子，重寫為情話

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你像鹽嗎？」
「為什麼？嫌我很鹹？」
「不是 —
少了你，
我整個人都沒味道。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給曖昧對象的土味情話，配晚餐照更自然
variations: 也可以改成糖版本：「你像糖 — 我本來以為自己很苦，遇到你才變甜」
降肉麻版：「你就是我的調味料，少了你我這道菜根本端不出去」

en.content:
"You know you're like salt?"
"Why, am I too much?"
"No —
without you,
I've got no flavor at all."
en.editorNote: Cheesy line to text someone you're into — pairs well with a dinner photo
variations: Sugar version: "You're like sugar — I thought I was bitter until you came along."
Lower the cheese: "You're my seasoning. Without you I'm a dish nobody would order."

items.sourceNote: everything's gonna brie all right 起司梗改寫為厭世語錄

zh-tw.content:
主管說：「別擔心，事情總會好起來的。」

我看著手上這塊融到爛的起司想：
是啊，
再爛的起司，
加熱一下還是有人會吃。

我大概就是那塊起司。
zh-tw.editorNote: 加班被主管畫大餅時的內心 OS，適合配便利商店熱壓吐司照
variations: 也可以接：「差別是起司融化還會牽絲，我融化只會崩潰」
降厭世版：「至少我跟起司一樣 — 越熱越有存在感」

en.content:
Boss: "Don't worry, everything's gonna be all right."

I stare at the melted cheese in my hand and think:
sure —
even the sloppiest cheese,
heat it up and someone'll still eat it.

I'm probably that cheese.
en.editorNote: Inner monologue when your boss feeds you empty reassurance during overtime
variations: Add: "Difference is, cheese melts into a nice stretch. I just melt down."
Less bleak: "At least I'm like cheese — the more heat, the harder I am to ignore."

items.sourceNote: 丸子『出來丸（玩）』夏天夜市諧音梗改寫

zh-tw.content:
夏天的夜市裡，哪種食物最愛揪人？

貢丸。
因為它整天都在喊 —
「出來丸（玩）啊！」
zh-tw.editorNote: 揪朋友逛夜市時丟群組破冰，夏天限定
variations: 也可以接：「結果一出門就被熱到只想滾回家」
邊緣人版本：「我回它：丸（玩）不動了，我選擇在家當冰」

en.content:
At a summer night market, which food is the biggest extrovert?

The meatball.
It keeps rolling up to everyone going —
"Come on, let's roll out!"
en.editorNote: Use it to rally friends for a night-market run — summer only
variations: Add: "Then we step outside, the heat hits, and we all roll right back home."
Introvert version: "I told it I can't roll today — I'm staying in as a popsicle."

items.sourceNote: 牛排『不熟』諧音梗改寫 — 經典三分熟梗重寫成上班場景

zh-tw.content:
三分熟的牛排在路上遇到五分熟的牛排，
卻誰也沒理誰。

為什麼？
因為他們不熟。

後來我才懂 —
我跟同事也是這樣熟的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 辦公室小團體文化的厭世吐槽，茶水間遇到不熟同事時心裡 OS
variations: 也可以接：「全熟那塊更慘，熟到沒人想咬」
離職版：「離職那天才發現，原來我跟整間公司都不熟」

en.content:
A rare steak and a medium steak pass each other on the street
and neither says hi.

Why?
Because they're not well done — they're not close.

Took me years to realize
that's exactly how I know my coworkers.
en.editorNote: Office small-talk dread — the mental note you make passing a coworker you've never actually clicked with
variations: Add: "The well-done one had it worse — so done nobody wanted a bite."
Quitting version: "Found out on my last day I was never really close with the whole company."

items.sourceNote: impasta（假冒義大利麵）諧音梗改寫成擬人台詞

zh-tw.content:
假裝自己是義大利麵的，叫什麼？

冒牌麵。

它最常說的一句話是 —
「我不是裝的，我只是還沒煮熟。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 吐槽職場上裝忙裝厲害的人，群組丟梗剛好
variations: 也可以接：「結果一下鍋就現形，軟掉了」
自嘲版：「我也是冒牌麵，假裝有在進步，其實還生的」

en.content:
What do you call pasta pretending to be the real deal?

An impasta.

Its favorite line is —
"I'm not faking it, I'm just not done cooking yet."
en.editorNote: For calling out the people at work who fake busy and fake competent
variations: Add: "Then it hit the boiling water and went soft instantly."
Self-roast version: "I'm an impasta too — pretending to grow, still totally raw."

items.sourceNote: seafood diet（我看到食物就吃）梗改寫成減肥情境

zh-tw.content:
朋友說他在執行「看到飲食法」。
我問是什麼，

他：「很簡單 —
看到食物，我就吃。」

難怪他減肥三年，體重很穩定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 減肥失敗自嘲首選，貼在限動配宵夜照超有共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「穩定到我懷疑他根本沒在減」
我的版本：「我是『聞到飲食法』，聞到香味就餓」

en.content:
My friend says he's on the "see-food diet."
I asked what that means.

Him: "Real simple —
I see food, I eat it."

No wonder his weight's been rock steady for three years.
en.editorNote: Top-tier self-roast for failed dieting — caption it over a late-night snack pic
variations: Add: "So steady I'm not convinced he's dieting at all."
My version: "I'm on the smell-food diet — one whiff and I'm starving."

items.sourceNote: 地瓜葉（yeah）諧音梗改寫成情緒對話格式

zh-tw.content:
我：「地瓜開心的時候會變成什麼？」
同事：「不知道。」
我：「地瓜葉（yeah）。」
同事：「那它不開心呢？」
我：「……就是現在的我，整個人爛在土裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一上班沒電時丟群組，諧音梗加厭世收尾雙重攻擊
variations: 也可以接：「而且還被當成便宜的配菜」
加班版：「老闆開心是地瓜葉，我加班是地瓜 — 被埋著」

en.content:
Me: "What does a sweet potato become when it's happy?"
Coworker: "No idea."
Me: "It goes 'yeah!' — sweet potato leaf."
Coworker: "And when it's sad?"
Me: "…that's just me right now, rotting in the dirt."
en.editorNote: Drop it in the group chat on a low-battery Monday — pun plus existential dread, double hit
variations: Add: "And it gets treated as the cheap side dish too."
Overtime version: "Boss is the happy leaf; I'm the buried potato pulling the late shift."

items.sourceNote: depresso（depressed + espresso）諧音梗改寫成上班族對話

zh-tw.content:
同事問我今天想喝什麼咖啡。
我：「一杯『憂鬱濃縮』。」
他：「沒這種品項。」
我：「有啊，espresso 前面加上我的人生狀態就是了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上點咖啡時丟給同事，配一張黑咖啡照超應景
variations: 也可以接：「續杯的話請幫我加倍憂鬱」
加班版：「我要『超時拿鐵』 — 奶泡是我的眼淚」

en.content:
A coworker asked what coffee I wanted today.
Me: "One depresso, please."
Him: "That's not on the menu."
Me: "Sure it is — it's just an espresso with my current mood added."
en.editorNote: Drop it at the Monday morning coffee run — pairs perfectly with a black coffee pic
variations: Add: "And make the refill a double on the sadness."
Overtime version: "I'll take an overtime latte — the foam is my tears."

items.sourceNote: nacho cheese（not your cheese）諧音梗改寫成室友搶食情境

zh-tw.content:
室友偷吃我冰箱裡的起司，我抓包他。
他：「這是誰的起司？」
我：「不是你的（nacho）。」
他：「那就是 nacho cheese 囉？」
我：「對，所以放回去。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼在室友群組宣示主權用，搭配冰箱貼紙照更有戲
variations: 也可以接：「下次再偷吃，整盒變成你的醫藥費」
辦公室版：「公司冰箱的便當 — 永遠都是 nacho lunch」

en.content:
Caught my roommate sneaking cheese from my fridge.
Him: "Whose cheese is this anyway?"
Me: "Nacho cheese."
Him: "So it IS nacho cheese?"
Me: "Exactly. So put it back."
en.editorNote: Post it in the roommate chat to stake your claim — bonus points with a fridge-sticker pic
variations: Add: "Steal it again and the whole block becomes your medical bill."
Office version: "The lunch in the work fridge — always nacho lunch."

items.sourceNote: eggs crack under pressure 梗改寫成擬人對話格式

zh-tw.content:
我問蛋：「你怎麼都不跟我說秘密？」
蛋：「因為我一有壓力就會裂開。」
我：「……我懂，我們週一都這樣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 壓力大的時候自嘲用，配一顆裂掉的蛋照超有共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「而且裂開以後還要被煎」
考試版：「我跟水煮蛋一樣 — 外表撐住，裡面早就熟透爛掉」

en.content:
I asked the egg: "Why won't you ever tell me your secrets?"
Egg: "Because the moment there's pressure, I crack."
Me: "…yeah, I get it. Same every Monday."
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating go-to for stressful days — caption it over a cracked-egg pic
variations: Add: "And after I crack, I still get fried."
Exam version: "I'm a hard-boiled egg — holding it together outside, fully done in inside."

items.sourceNote: lettuce turnip the beet 連續蔬菜諧音梗改寫成揪人運動情境

zh-tw.content:
我傳訊息揪朋友去跳舞：「走啦，讓我們把節奏炒起來！」
朋友：「我才剛下班，全身像被燙過的青菜。」
我：「那正好，先把你『回鍋』一下就有體力了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 揪人運動或夜衝被已讀時丟出來，土到對方不得不回
variations: 也可以接：「不去的話，下次聚餐你就是被冰起來的隔夜菜」
減肥版：「不動的青菜，最後都會變成我肚子上的肉」

en.content:
Texted a friend to go dancing: "Come on, lettuce turn up the beat!"
Friend: "I just got off work, I'm wilted like overcooked greens."
Me: "Perfect — we'll just stir-fry you back to life first."
en.editorNote: Fire it off when your workout invite gets left on read — too cheesy to ignore
variations: Add: "Skip it and you're the sad leftovers nobody reheats."
Diet version: "Veggies that never move end up as the weight on my belly."

items.sourceNote: 披薩笑話梗改寫參考

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「你怎麼看老闆昨天那個提案？」
我：「就像叫外送披薩 —
講得口沫橫飛，
真的送到，
只剩一片。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 開會後跟同事私下吐槽畫大餅主管時丟出來，安全又解氣
variations: 也可以接：「而且那一片還是冷的」
加薪版：「年終就像披薩優惠券，看得到、用不到、還有期限」

en.content:
Coworker: "So what'd you think of the boss's big pitch yesterday?"
Me: "It was like ordering delivery pizza —
sounds huge on the phone,
then it shows up
and it's one slice."
en.editorNote: Vent to a coworker after a meeting full of empty promises — safe and satisfying
variations: Add: "And that one slice was cold."
Bonus version: "The year-end bonus is like a pizza coupon — you see it, you can't use it, and it expires."

items.sourceNote: 咖啡諧音梗改寫參考

zh-tw.content:
早上進辦公室，同事看我一臉死氣。
她：「你需要一杯咖啡。」
我：「我需要的是報案 —
我的睡眠被搶了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上進公司放群組，配一張黑眼圈自拍最有感
variations: 也可以回：「咖啡救不了我，它自己也是被榨出來的」
加班版：「這杯不是提神，是續命」

en.content:
Walked into the office looking half-dead.
Coworker: "You need a coffee."
Me: "What I need is to file a report —
somebody mugged my sleep."
en.editorNote: Drop it in the group chat on a Monday morning, ideally with a puffy-eyed selfie
variations: Reply: "Coffee can't save me — it got squeezed dry too."
Overtime version: "This cup isn't a pick-me-up, it's life support."

items.sourceNote: 蔬菜勵志諧音梗改寫參考

zh-tw.content:
朋友低潮，我想安慰他。
我：「你就像顆胡蘿蔔 —
就算被埋在土裡，
也是那片田裡最出色的。」
朋友：「所以你是說我土？」
我：「……重點是出色。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 想鼓勵人又不想太肉麻時用，被吐槽也能笑著帶過
variations: 也可以改洋蔥版：「你不是愛哭，你只是層次比較多」
感情版：「我對你的思念，一天比一天『菜』（在意）」

en.content:
My friend was down, so I tried to cheer him up.
Me: "You're like a carrot —
even buried in the dirt,
you're still outstanding in your field."
Friend: "So you're saying I'm dirty?"
Me: "...The point was outstanding."
en.editorNote: For when you want to encourage someone without getting too mushy — survives the eye-roll
variations: Onion version: "You're not crybaby — you just have layers."
Romantic version: "You make my heart turnip every time."

items.sourceNote: 麵包諧音梗改寫參考

zh-tw.content:
麵包對奶油告白：「有你在，我整個人生都圓滿了。」
奶油：「你只是餓了吧？」
麵包：「不是 —
沒有你，
我就只是一塊乾巴巴的吐司。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 情人節或紀念日想撒糖又想搞笑時用，甜中帶土
variations: 也可以接吐司回：「那我塗上去，我們就『一拍即合』」
單身版：「所以我到現在還是一塊沒抹醬的乾吐司」

en.content:
The bread confessed to the butter: "With you, my whole life feels complete."
Butter: "You're just hungry, aren't you?"
Bread: "No —
without you,
I'm just a dry, sad slice of toast."
en.editorNote: For Valentine's or an anniversary when you want sweet with a side of corny
variations: Butter's reply: "Fine, spread me on — we're on a roll now."
Single version: "Which explains why I'm still a plain, unbuttered slice."

items.sourceNote: 玉米諧音梗改寫參考

zh-tw.content:
我：「你知道玉米最愛講什麼笑話嗎？」
同事：「不知道。」
我：「冷笑話 —
因為它每一句都很『粒』（力）不從心。」
同事默默把玉米從便當夾走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 午餐時想活絡氣氛用，冷到對方翻白眼就是成功
variations: 也可以接：「而且它笑話講完，大家都會『玉』言又止」
爆米花版：「一緊張就爆開，跟我上台報告一樣」

en.content:
Me: "You know what kind of jokes corn loves best?"
Coworker: "No idea."
Me: "Corny ones —
every punchline is a-maize-ing."
Coworker quietly moves the corn off my lunch tray.
en.editorNote: Use it over lunch to break the ice — if they groan, you nailed it
variations: Add: "And when it bombs, the whole room gets ear-ie silence."
Popcorn version: "One bit of pressure and it pops — just like me before a presentation."

items.sourceNote: 豆腐諧音梗原創格式

zh-tw.content:
同事失戀好幾天，我端了盤麻婆豆腐給他。
他：「你什麼意思？」
我：「別難過了 — 這年頭，連豆腐都要靠麻辣才紅，你也可以重新調味啊。」
他一口氣把整盤吃光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 安慰朋友又想耍冷時用，療癒指數靠一盤豆腐撐場
variations: 也可以接：「而且豆腐最厲害，被壓成什麼樣都還是自己」
臭豆腐版：「越臭越多人排隊，做人有時候也要有點個性」

en.content:
My coworker had been heartbroken for days, so I brought him a plate of mapo tofu.
Him: "What's that supposed to mean?"
Me: "Cheer up — even tofu needs a little spice to get noticed. You can re-season your life too."
He finished the whole plate in one go.
en.editorNote: Use it to comfort a friend while still being cheesy — one plate of tofu does the heavy lifting
variations: Add: "Besides, tofu keeps it together no matter how hard life presses it."
Stinky tofu version: "The stinkier it gets, the longer the line — sometimes you just need more character."

items.sourceNote: 夜市珍奶對話原創格式

zh-tw.content:
夜市飲料攤。
我：「珍奶去冰半糖。」
老闆：「珍珠要不要多加？」
我：「不用，我怕胖。」
老闆：「妹妹，妳都點半糖了，命運早就決定妳會回來加珍珠。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 點半糖又怕胖的日常，發限動配一杯珍奶超有共鳴
variations: 也可以接：「果然十分鐘後我又走回去了」
微糖版：「老闆說微糖是給還想騙自己的人喝的」

en.content:
At a night market drink stall.
Me: "Bubble tea, less ice, half sugar."
Owner: "Extra pearls?"
Me: "Nah, watching my weight."
Owner: "Sweetie, you already ordered half sugar. Fate has decided you'll be back for the pearls."
en.editorNote: Perfect for the classic 'half sugar but scared of calories' story — pairs great with a bubble tea photo
variations: Add: "Sure enough, ten minutes later I walked right back."
Quarter-sugar version: "Owner said quarter sugar is for people still trying to fool themselves."

items.sourceNote: 雞排擬人格式原創

zh-tw.content:
雞排對排在後面的雞塊說：
「我勸你別排了。」
雞塊：「為什麼？」
雞排：「進去這鍋，出來就變成別人的宵夜。
我們雞生，最怕的就是 — 太酥了讓人上癮。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 宵夜時間發文用，配一塊剛炸好的雞排照最對味
variations: 也可以接：「而且撒上椒鹽那刻，我們就再也回不去了」
鹹酥雞版：「一整籃下去，沒有一塊能全身而退」

en.content:
A fried chicken cutlet says to the nuggets waiting behind it:
"Trust me, don't get in that line."
Nugget: "Why not?"
Cutlet: "You go into that oil, you come out as somebody's midnight snack.
The scariest part of being chicken — we're just too crispy to resist."
en.editorNote: Post it at midnight-snack o'clock, ideally next to a fresh fried cutlet
variations: Add: "And the second they hit the pepper salt, there's no going back."
Popcorn chicken version: "A whole basket goes in, and not one piece makes it out alive."

items.sourceNote: 滷肉飯諧音梗原創

zh-tw.content:
我：「你知道滷肉飯為什麼從來不生氣嗎？」
朋友：「為什麼？」
我：「因為它超會『滷』（忍）—
再大的火，它都撐得住，最後還香噴噴。」
朋友把最後一口飯讓給我，說我值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想安慰壓力大的朋友又要耍冷時用，滷肉飯配一句諧音剛剛好
variations: 也可以接：「而且它越滷越入味，人生也是熬出來的」
爌肉飯版：「它躺那麼大一塊，就是懂得放鬆」

en.content:
Me: "You know why braised pork rice never gets angry?"
Friend: "Why?"
Me: "Because it knows how to simmer down —
no matter how high the heat, it just gets more flavorful."
My friend gave me the last bite and said I earned it.
en.editorNote: Use it to comfort a stressed friend while staying pun-y — braised pork rice plus a groaner is the right ratio
variations: Add: "The longer it braises, the deeper the flavor — life's kind of the same."
Pork belly version: "It lies there in one big slab because it truly knows how to relax."

items.sourceNote: 披薩誠實諧音梗 — 網路流行冷笑話重寫

zh-tw.content:
我：「你知道全世界最誠實的食物是什麼嗎？」
同事：「什麼？」
我：「披薩。」
同事：「為什麼？」
我：「因為它只有四片、六片、八片 — 從來不會『七』（欺）騙你。」
同事默默把最後一片拿走，說這樣才叫誠實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 訂披薩開會分食時講最應景，講完通常會被搶走最後一片
variations: 也可以接：「不像便當，菜色寫得漂亮，打開全是想像」
手搖版：「珍奶最不誠實 — 說是半糖，甜到我懷疑人生」

en.content:
Me: "You know the most honest food in the world?"
Coworker: "What?"
Me: "Pizza."
Coworker: "Why?"
Me: "Because it only comes in four, six, or eight slices — it never tries to short-change you."
My coworker quietly took the last slice, said that's what honesty looks like.
en.editorNote: Best told while splitting a pizza at a meeting — someone always grabs the last slice right after
variations: Add: "Unlike a lunchbox — the menu looks great, you open it, and it's all imagination."
Boba version: the least honest food is bubble tea — you order half sugar and question your whole life

items.sourceNote: 起司樂觀諧音梗 — everything's gonna brie all right 中英雙關重寫

zh-tw.content:
朋友焦慮到睡不著，問我人生會不會好起來。
我遞給他一片起司說：
「你看它，被壓成這樣還是這麼香。
別擔心，一切都會『起司』（起色）的。」
他笑到把那片起司吃掉，說好像真的沒那麼糟了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜安慰崩潰朋友時用，配一片起司比雞湯還有效
variations: 也可以接：「而且它越放越入味，你也是」
焗烤版：「人生就像焗烤 — 最上面燒焦一點，底下反而最好吃」

en.content:
A friend was too anxious to sleep, asked if life would ever get better.
I handed him a slice of cheese and said:
"Look at it — pressed flat like that and still this good.
Don't worry. Everything's gonna brie all right."
He laughed so hard he ate the cheese, said maybe it wasn't so bad after all.
en.editorNote: For comforting a spiraling friend at midnight — one slice of cheese beats a whole pep talk
variations: Add: "And the longer it sits, the better it gets — same goes for you."
Casserole version: life is like a bake — a little burnt on top, and the bottom's the best part

items.sourceNote: 海鮮減肥梗 — seafood diet: I see food and I eat it 重寫

zh-tw.content:
健身教練問我最近在吃什麼。
我：「海鮮餐。」
教練：「不錯啊，很健康。」
我：「不是那種 — 是『看』到什麼吃什麼，海鮮的海就是『看』（see food）。」
教練把課表撕了，說先從心態開始救。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被人逼問減肥進度時的完美擋箭牌，講完可以繼續吃
variations: 也可以接：「所以夜市對我來說是自助餐」
低卡版：「我吃很清淡啊，清淡到只剩鹹酥雞的鹹」

en.content:
My trainer asked what I've been eating lately.
Me: "A seafood diet."
Trainer: "Nice, that's healthy."
Me: "Not that kind — I see food, and I eat it."
My trainer tore up my workout plan, said we'd start with the mindset first.
en.editorNote: The perfect shield when someone grills you about your diet progress — then keep eating
variations: Add: "Which means the night market is basically an all-you-can-eat buffet to me."
Low-cal version: "I eat really light — light enough that only the salt on the fried chicken is left."

items.sourceNote: 香料時間就是金錢 — thyme is money 中英雙關重寫

zh-tw.content:
同事抱怨加班沒加班費。
我：「你知道為什麼香料店最貴的是百里香嗎？」
同事：「為什麼？」
我：「因為『時間就是金錢』，而百里香的香就是 time（thyme）。
你的時間被公司免費拿走，難怪這麼虧。」
同事當場提了離職，說被一根香料點醒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在辦公室吐槽血汗加班時丟出來，冷到同事清醒
variations: 也可以接：「所以下次加班，記得先秤秤自己值多少香料」
老闆版：「老闆說共體時艱，其實是想省下你那把百里香」

en.content:
A coworker griped about unpaid overtime.
Me: "You know why thyme is the priciest herb in the shop?"
Coworker: "Why?"
Me: "Because time is money — and the company's been taking yours for free.
No wonder you feel robbed."
My coworker quit on the spot, said one herb had woken them up.
en.editorNote: Drop it in the office when someone's ranting about sweatshop overtime — cold enough to snap them awake
variations: Add: "So next time you stay late, weigh how much thyme you're actually worth."
Boss version: when the boss says 'we're all in this together,' he really means he's saving on your thyme

items.sourceNote: 地瓜諧音梗 — 你蕃薯了 網路流行梗重寫

zh-tw.content:
夜市買烤地瓜，老闆多塞一顆給我。
我：「這樣不會虧本嗎？」
老闆：「做生意不能算太細 — 太計較就『輸』了。」
我：「所以你是故意讓利？」
老闆：「對，我寧願『蕃薯』（翻輸為贏），也不要小氣到讓客人跑掉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 分享暖心小店故事時用，諧音收尾讓溫馨不肉麻
variations: 也可以接：「結果我隔天又去買，他又多塞一顆 — 這波他真的贏了」
早餐店版：「老闆娘多給一顆蛋，說『加減賺』比較實在」

en.content:
Bought a roasted sweet potato at the night market, and the owner tossed in an extra one.
Me: "Won't you lose money doing that?"
Owner: "You can't run a shop counting every cent — count too hard and you've already lost."
Me: "So you're giving stuff away on purpose?"
Owner: "Sure. I'd rather give a little than be so stingy the customers walk off."
en.editorNote: Great for sharing a warm little-shop story — the pun ending keeps it sweet without getting sappy
variations: Add: "I went back the next day and he slipped me another one — that round, he definitely won."
Breakfast shop version: the owner throws in an extra egg, says 'a little extra keeps folks coming back'

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 為什麼吐司永遠交不到女朋友？因為它總是被烤（靠）邊站。
zh-tw.editorNote: 早餐店閒聊、烘焙話題開場的冷笑話
variations: 為什麼吐司這麼孤單？因為它老是被烤邊。

en.content: Why did the loaf of bread stay single? It never found anyone it could rise to the occasion with.
en.editorNote: Light opener for a bakery or brunch chat
variations: Why is bread bad at dating? It always ends up toast.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 荷包蛋去看醫生，醫生說：「你這是煎熬（煎熬）過度啦。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊早餐或壓力大時的諧音梗
variations: 荷包蛋最近壓力好大，因為每天都在被煎熬。

en.content: I asked the fried egg how it was doing. It said, "Honestly? I'm a little cracked under pressure."
en.editorNote: Breakfast small talk or a pun about stress
variations: The fried egg quit its job. It couldn't take being flipped around anymore.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 珍珠奶茶為什麼這麼受歡迎？因為它從不吝嗇給你滿滿的珍珠，不像有些人只會給你空頭支票。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊飲料或台灣手搖店時的自嘲式笑點
variations: 珍奶最誠實了，說有珍珠就真的有珍珠。

en.content: Why is bubble tea so trustworthy? When it promises you pearls, it actually delivers—unlike my ex.
en.editorNote: Casual joke over drinks or about boba culture
variations: Bubble tea never lets me down. Every sip's got a surprise.

items.sourceNote: internal only — nacho cheese pun 改寫

zh-tw.content:
冰箱裡有塊起司，我伸手要拿。
室友馬上大喊：「那不是你的！」
我：「所以它叫『拿錯起司』？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 共用冰箱被抓包偷吃時的緩頰台詞
variations: 也可以回：「我以為是公用的，結果是『公道自在人心』的公。」

en.content:
There was one slice of cheese left in the fridge, so I reached for it.
My roommate yelled, "That's not yours!"
Me: "So it's... nacho cheese?"
en.editorNote: Deflection line for getting caught raiding the shared fridge
variations: Follow-up: "Relax, I was just gouda-ing my options."

items.sourceNote: internal only — pizza chef delivery pun 改寫

zh-tw.content:
披薩店老闆改行去說相聲。
大家問他為什麼這麼有把握。
他：「我做外送十年，最強的就是『準時交（笑）點』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊轉職或副業時可以拿來自嘲的冷笑話
variations: 也可以說：「他上台不緊張，反正客訴聽多了，噓聲對他來說是背景音。」

en.content:
The pizza guy quit to become a stand-up comedian.
People asked how he was so confident.
Him: "Ten years of delivery. Nobody nails timing like me."
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating line for career-change or side-hustle chats
variations: Add: "Worst case, I just fold under pressure—like the box."

items.sourceNote: internal only — seafood diet pun 改寫

zh-tw.content:
同事問我在減肥嗎，看我盤子堆這麼高。
我：「對啊，我吃的是『看到就吃』飲食法 — 看到什麼，就吃什麼。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 自助餐或吃到飽被虧食量大時的救場台詞
variations: 英文原梗是「seafood diet（see food, eat it）」，中文可玩成「見食法」。

en.content:
A coworker saw my loaded plate and asked if I was on a diet.
Me: "Yeah—the seafood diet. I see food, and I eat it."
en.editorNote: Save-the-moment line at a buffet when someone side-eyes your portions
variations: Follow-up: "Started strong this morning. Then I saw breakfast."

items.sourceNote: internal only — weir-dough / kneaded dough pun 改寫

zh-tw.content:
我朋友開披薩店開到快倒了。
我問他問題出在哪。
他：「客人太少，麵團天天剩，我現在最缺的就是『捏（麵）錢』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊創業辛酸或生意冷清時的苦笑梗
variations: 也可以說：「不喜歡披薩的人都很怪 — 他們是『怪麵團』。」

en.content:
My buddy's pizzeria is barely surviving.
I asked what went wrong.
Him: "Not enough customers—I literally kneaded the dough more than I could make it."
en.editorNote: Wry line for chats about failing small businesses or slow days
variations: Add: "People who hate pizza? Total weir-doughs."

items.sourceNote: internal only — espresso / express 諧音梗改寫成台式咖啡店場景

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「你對這份工作到底還有沒有熱情？」
我指著手上那杯咖啡說：
「我對它的愛，濃到 Espresso 都表達不出來。對工作嘛 — 早就冷掉了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 週一早上在辦公室廚房煮咖啡時的自嘲台詞，配一杯黑咖啡照發限動很搭
variations: 也可以回主管：「我不是沒精神，我只是還沒 Espresso 完自己。」
失戀版：「他走了以後，我對誰都表達不出 Espresso 了。」

en.content:
A coworker asked if I still had any passion left for this job.
I held up my coffee and said:
"My love for this cup? I can't espresso it. For the job? That went cold hours ago."
en.editorNote: A self-deprecating line for the Monday office kitchen, pairs well with a black-coffee story photo
variations: Reply to your boss: "I'm not low-energy, I just haven't espresso'd myself yet."
Breakup version: "After they left, I couldn't espresso my feelings to anyone."

items.sourceNote: internal only — sushi "roll with it" 梗改寫成台灣壽司店對話

zh-tw.content:
我在壽司店跟師傅抱怨最近人生很卡。
師傅一邊捲手卷一邊說：
「人生就跟壽司一樣啦 — 該捲的時候就捲，別跟它硬碰硬，不然只會散成一盤飯。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友遇到低潮時分享，帶點溫度的療癒系食物哲學
variations: 簡短版：「人生卡住的時候，記得像壽司一樣 — 捲起來繼續走。」
職場版：「開會被電就當作在捲壽司 — 捲一捲，一口吞下去就過了。」

en.content:
I was griping to the sushi chef about how stuck my life feels lately.
He kept rolling a hand roll and said:
"Life's like sushi—when it's time to roll, you roll. Fight it and you just end up as a scattered plate of rice."
en.editorNote: A gently comforting food-philosophy line to share when a friend is going through a rough patch
variations: Short version: "When life gets stuck, just roll with it—like sushi."
Work version: "Got grilled in the meeting? Treat it like rolling sushi—wrap it up and swallow it in one bite."

items.sourceNote: internal only — bread "kneaded help / therapy" 梗改寫成麵包店擬人

zh-tw.content:
麵包去看心理諮商。
諮商師問：「你最近怎麼了？」
麵包嘆氣：「我壓力大到快發不起來，我真的很需要被人好好『揉』一下。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班壓力大、需要被安慰時的可愛擬人梗，配剛出爐麵包照很療癒
variations: 續集：「諮商師說我想太多，其實我只是在『鬧（麵）脾氣』。」
情侶版：「你就是我的吐司 — 我對你只有滿滿的『土思（吐司）』。」

en.content:
A loaf of bread went to therapy.
The therapist asked, "So, what's been going on?"
The bread sighed: "I'm under so much pressure I can barely rise. I really just kneaded someone to hold me."
en.editorNote: A cute personification bit for when work stress is piling up, adorable with a fresh-from-the-oven bread photo
variations: Follow-up: "The therapist said I overthink. Honestly, I'm just a little crumby lately."
Couple version: "You're my slice of toast—I loaf you a hole lot."

items.sourceNote: internal only — taco "spill the beans" 梗改寫成夜市對話

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我：「這麼多小吃，你為什麼最不敢相信賣墨西哥捲的那攤？」
我：「因為它裡面全是豆子啊 — 有秘密它一定藏不住，一咬就『爆料』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 跟愛講八卦的朋友互虧時可以丟出來的諧音梗
variations: 反過來吐槽自己：「別跟我講秘密，我就是那捲墨西哥捲 — 一緊張就爆餡。」
夜市版可換成刈包：「刈包看起來嘴很緊，其實一張口全掉出來。」

en.content:
A friend asked, "Out of all the street food, why do you trust the taco stand the least?"
Me: "Because it's full of beans—it can't keep a single secret. One bite and it spills everything."
en.editorNote: A pun to lob at the friend who can never keep gossip to themselves
variations: Turn it on yourself: "Don't tell me secrets—I'm basically a taco. One nervous bite and the filling's everywhere."
Swap in a burrito: "A burrito looks tight-lipped, but open it up and it all falls out."

items.sourceNote: internal only — 咖啡 mugged/被搶 諧音梗改寫成台灣早餐店情境

zh-tw.content:
早餐店的美式咖啡去警局報案。
警察問：「發生什麼事？」
它說：「我被人裝進杯子裡帶走了 — 標準的『杯』劫。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配一杯早餐店咖啡的照片發限動，早上想討拍時用
variations: 拿鐵版本：「我本來好好的，結果被加了一堆奶泡壓在最上面 — 這叫『鮮奶霸凌』。」
上班族版：「我的咖啡跟我一樣，一大早就被綁架到公司。」

en.content:
The breakfast-shop coffee went to file a police report.
Cop: "What happened?"
Coffee: "Somebody stuffed me in a cup and carried me off. Classic case of getting mugged."
en.editorNote: Post with a photo of your morning cup when you want sympathy before work
variations: Latte version: "I was fine, then they piled foam on top and pinned me down—straight-up milk bullying."
Office version: "My coffee's like me—kidnapped to the office first thing in the morning."

items.sourceNote: internal only — cheese "everything's gonna brie all right" 樂觀起司梗改寫

zh-tw.content:
我問一塊起司：「你怎麼永遠都不會焦慮？」
它說：「因為我知道 — 就算被融化、被切片、被夾進漢堡，最後大家還是超愛我。」
我沉默了。連起司都比我懂得被討厭也沒關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 厭世但療癒的一則，適合週一憂鬱時發，配起司漢堡照更好
variations: 自嘲版：「我跟起司唯一的差別是 — 它被融化會變好吃，我被融化只會遲到。」
打氣版：「就算今天被生活切成片，你還是很多人心中的靈魂配料。」

en.content:
I asked a slice of cheese, "How are you never anxious?"
It said, "Because I know that even melted, sliced, and shoved in a burger, everyone still loves me."
I went quiet. Even cheese has made peace with being messed with.
en.editorNote: Bittersweet but comforting—good for a Monday slump, better with a cheeseburger photo
variations: Self-deprecating: "The only difference between me and cheese is—melt it and it gets tastier; melt me and I just show up late."
Pep-talk: "Even if life slices you thin today, you're still someone's favorite topping."

items.sourceNote: internal only — bread "too kneady" 麵包太黏人梗改寫成感情吐槽

zh-tw.content:
閨蜜問我為什麼跟那個開麵包店的分手。
我：「他每天都要黏著我、揉來揉去、確認我有沒有膨脹。」
她：「那不是很甜嗎？」
我：「他把我當麵團在發，不是在談戀愛。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合姐妹聊感情、吐槽太黏的另一半時丟出來
variations: 反向版：「我才是那個麵團 — 沒被關心就一直塌下去。」
職場版：「我主管管我像揉麵團，每天都要確認我有沒有『發』。」

en.content:
My bestie asked why I broke up with the guy who runs a bakery.
Me: "He clung to me every day, kept kneading me, checking if I'd risen."
Her: "Isn't that sweet?"
Me: "He was proofing dough, not dating me. Way too kneady."
en.editorNote: Drop it when venting with friends about a partner who's too clingy
variations: Flip it: "Honestly I'm the dough—leave me unattended and I just deflate."
Work version: "My boss handles me like dough—every day checking whether I've risen."

items.sourceNote: internal only — eggs "crack under pressure / can't keep secrets" 蛋守不住秘密梗改寫成夜市情境

zh-tw.content:
我問夜市賣蔥抓餅的：「為什麼你都不敢跟蛋講秘密？」
老闆頭也不抬：「因為它一有壓力就裂開，還會把裡面全部倒出來 — 跟我前女友一模一樣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夜市排隊等餐時可以跟朋友聊的諧音梗，老闆的補刀是精華
variations: 自嘲版：「別給我壓力，我就是那顆蛋 — 一被念就當場裂給你看。」
辦公室版：「開會不要問我意見，我一緊張就跟水煮蛋一樣，殼一敲全露。」

en.content:
I asked the night-market scallion-pancake guy, "Why won't you tell an egg your secrets?"
He didn't even look up: "Because it cracks under the slightest pressure and dumps everything out. Exactly like my ex."
en.editorNote: A pun for the line at a food stall—the vendor's follow-up is the whole joke
variations: Self-deprecating: "Don't pressure me—I'm the egg. Nag me once and I crack on the spot."
Office version: "Don't ask my opinion in meetings—I get nervous like a boiled egg: one tap and the shell's off."

items.sourceNote: internal only — "seafood diet: I see food and I eat it" 經典梗改寫成台灣減肥情境

zh-tw.content:
同事問我最近在減肥吃什麼。
我：「我在執行『看見食物』飲食法。」
她眼睛一亮：「聽起來很專業，怎麼吃？」
我：「就是 — 看見食物，我就吃。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 揪吃飯被念太胖時的完美回擊，飯局群組必發
variations: 加碼版：「我還有搭配運動 — 運動嘴巴。」
夜市版：「我這叫地中海飲食 — 從夜市這頭吃到那頭，中間都不能停。」

en.content:
A coworker asked what diet I'm on lately.
Me: "I'm doing the see-food diet."
Her eyes lit up: "Sounds fancy—how does it work?"
Me: "Simple. I see food, and I eat it."
en.editorNote: The perfect comeback when someone nags you about eating—drop it in the group chat before a meal
variations: Add-on: "I pair it with exercise too—jaw exercise."
Night-market version: "Mine's the marathon diet—eat from one end of the market to the other, no stopping in between."

items.sourceNote: internal only — coffee "espresso yourself / strong and bitter" 咖啡梗改寫成安慰失戀情境

zh-tw.content:
朋友失戀，我請他喝咖啡安慰。
他盯著杯子說：「你看，連 Espresso 都懂我 — 又濃又苦，還小小一杯。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 安慰失戀朋友時的黑色幽默，配一杯 espresso 拍照發限動剛好
variations: 加碼版：「加了奶泡以後看起來人模人樣，跟我上班一樣。」
美式版：「我比較像美式 — 看起來很多，其實一半都是水。」

en.content:
A friend got dumped, so I took him out for coffee.
He stared into the cup: "See, even the espresso gets me — strong, bitter, and served in a tiny cup."
en.editorNote: Dark humor for cheering up a heartbroken friend—pair it with an actual espresso
variations: Add-on: "With some foam on top it looks presentable—just like me at work."
Americano version: "I'm more of an Americano—looks like a lot, but half of it's just water."

items.sourceNote: internal only — "nacho cheese = not your cheese" 經典諧音改寫成台灣室友冰箱情境

zh-tw.content:
室友在冰箱貼紙條：「這塊起司是我的，誰偷吃就絕交。」
隔天起司少一角，他暴怒質問我。
我一臉無辜：「你冷靜，那不是你的起司 — 那是 nacho cheese，本來就 not-your cheese。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 跟愛貼冰箱紙條的室友互嗆時發，中英夾雜的諧音是精華
variations: 進階版：他問剩下的呢，我說那是 my-cho cheese。
辦公室版：公用零食被掃光，兇手一律回答『那不是你的，是大家的（nacho）』。

en.content:
My roommate taped a note on the fridge: "This cheese is MINE. Steal it and we're done."
Next day a corner was gone. He came at me furious.
Me, totally innocent: "Relax — that's not your cheese. That's nacho cheese."
en.editorNote: For roasting the roommate who labels everything in the shared fridge
variations: Follow-up: he asked where the rest went. I said, "That's my-cho cheese."
Office version: when the shared snacks vanish, the culprit always insists, "It wasn't yours—it was nacho... I mean, everyone's."

items.sourceNote: internal only — "I loaf you / fresh bread goes stale" 麵包梗改寫成土味情話互嗆

zh-tw.content:
男友半夜傳訊：「我對妳的愛，就像剛出爐的吐司。」
我：「鬆鬆軟軟？」
他：「不是 — 是放到隔天就會變硬，還得靠妳來 toast 我一下才有溫度。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合轉發到閨蜜群組吐槽另一半的土味情話
variations: 回擊版：「那我建議你直接買冷凍的，保存期限比較長。」
已婚版：「結婚十年，我們的愛從吐司變法國麵包 — 外硬，內也硬。」

en.content:
My boyfriend texts at midnight: "My love for you is like fresh-baked bread."
Me: "Warm and fluffy?"
Him: "Nope — goes stale by morning, and you've gotta toast me to get any warmth out."
en.editorNote: Forward it to the group chat to roast your partner's cheesy lines
variations: Comeback: "Then maybe get the frozen kind—longer shelf life."
Married version: "Ten years in, our love went from toast to a baguette—hard on the outside, hard on the inside too."

items.sourceNote: internal only — "batter wraps around everything" 概念改寫成台灣鹹酥雞攤暖心自嘲

zh-tw.content:
我問鹹酥雞老闆：「為什麼你炸的東西大家都愛？」
老闆邊甩油鍋邊說：「因為我做人跟炸粉一樣 — 願意把自己裹在別人身上，一起變得酥脆又搶手。」
我：「老闆你這番話…比九層塔還提味。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夜市排隊等鹹酥雞時跟老闆搭話的暖心幽默
variations: 厭世版：「我也想被裹粉下鍋 — 至少出來的時候大家都搶著要。」
加辣版：「我的人生就像加了辣椒粉 — 看起來紅紅火火，其實只是嗆。」

en.content:
I asked the fried-chicken stall guy why everyone loves his food.
Shaking the fryer, he said: "Because I'm like the batter — happy to wrap myself around others so we all come out crispy and in demand."
Me: "Sir... that hit harder than the basil."
en.editorNote: Warm humor for chatting up the vendor while waiting for late-night fried chicken
variations: Jaded version: "Honestly, I wish someone would batter and fry me—at least then people would fight over me."
Extra spicy: "My life's like a dusting of chili powder—looks fiery, but really it just stings."

items.sourceNote: internal only — pizza honesty 概念改寫成台灣人設對話

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「你覺得世界上最誠實的食物是什麼？」
我：「披薩。」
他：「為什麼？」
我：「因為它只會被切成四片、六片、八片 — 從來不會七片，絕對不欺騙你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 訂披薩開會的午休閒聊，適合丟到辦公室群組
variations: 延伸版：「不像我主管的 KPI，永遠算不出到底幾片是我的。」
厭世版：「至少披薩切幾片還會先問你，我加班從來沒人問過我。」

en.content:
A coworker asked me what the most honest food in the world is.
Me: "Pizza."
Him: "Why?"
Me: "It only ever comes in four, six, or eight slices — never seven. It would never cheat you."
en.editorNote: Small talk while a shared pizza shows up at the office
variations: Extension: "Unlike my manager's targets—I can never figure out how many slices are actually mine."
Jaded version: "At least the pizza asks how many slices you want. Nobody asks before piling on my overtime."

items.sourceNote: internal only — 丸子「出來丸」諧音概念重寫成擬人對話

zh-tw.content:
問：哪種食物最愛往外跑、最不想待在家？
答：丸子。
因為它整天喊著「我要出來丸（玩）」。
火鍋店老闆聽完默默把它撈起來：「好，那你先出鍋。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 吃火鍋等丸子熟的時候講給朋友聽的冷笑話
variations: 接龍版：「那貢丸勒？」「貢丸比較宅，它只想被『供』在家裡。」
厭世版：「我也想出來玩，可惜我是被生活煮熟的那顆。」

en.content:
Q: Which food most wants to go out and never stay home?
A: A meatball.
Because it keeps yelling, "Let me out, let me roll!"
The hot-pot cook quietly scoops it up: "Fine — you're out of the pot first."
en.editorNote: A groaner to drop while waiting for the meatballs to cook in hot pot
variations: Follow-up: "What about the fish ball?" "That one's a homebody—it just wants to stay in the broth."
Jaded version: "I'd love to go out and roll around too, but life already boiled me all the way through."

items.sourceNote: internal only — "I see food and I eat it" seafood diet 概念重寫成減肥對話

zh-tw.content:
朋友問：「你最近在減肥喔？吃什麼餐？」
我：「海鮮餐。」
她眼睛一亮：「哇好健康！」
我：「不是啦 — 是看到食物（see food）我就吃，看到什麼就是我的餐。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 被朋友問減肥進度時，自嘲用的萬用回答
variations: 加碼版：「而且我還很專一，從早餐吃到宵夜都沒變過心。」
健身房版：「教練說要控制飲食，我控制得很好 — 一口都沒放過。」

en.content:
A friend asked: "You on a diet lately? What kind?"
Me: "Seafood diet."
Her eyes lit up: "Ooh, so healthy!"
Me: "Not that kind — I see food, and I eat it. Whatever I see is the meal."
en.editorNote: The all-purpose comeback when someone asks how your diet's going
variations: Add-on: "And I'm loyal too—stuck with it from breakfast straight through to midnight snacks."
Gym version: "My trainer said watch what I eat. I watched every bite—didn't miss a single one."

items.sourceNote: internal only — "big dill / bacon my heart" 蔬果諧音土味情話重寫成台灣版

zh-tw.content:
他想追我，傳了一整排土味情話：
「妳是我的菜。」
「沒有妳我會很『菜』。」
「我願意為妳『青』出於藍。」
我回：「你這樣講，我只覺得你很『韭』沒談戀愛了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 閨蜜群組吐槽對方傳的蔬菜諧音情話
variations: 回擊版：「你再『菜』下去，我怕你連追人都要重修。」
已讀版：「這些梗我『蒜』了，下次直接請我吃飯比較實在。」

en.content:
A guy tried to win me over with a full lineup of veggie pickup lines:
"You're kind of a big dill."
"Don't go bacon my heart."
"We're mint to be."
I replied: "With lines this corny, I can tell it's bean way too long since your last date."
en.editorNote: For roasting the produce-aisle pickup lines in the group chat
variations: Comeback: "Any cheesier and you'll have to retake Flirting 101."
Left-on-read version: "I've herd enough—next time just buy me dinner, it's more a-peeling."

items.sourceNote: internal only — 麵團「被桿來桿去」概念重寫成職場擬人自嘲

zh-tw.content:
問：哪種食物的職場處境最慘？
答：麵團。
因為它每天都被人桿來桿去，從早桿到晚，還被嫌不夠平。
麵團嘆氣：「我不是不努力 — 是我這行本來就是攤（tan）著被壓的命。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 加班到懷疑人生時，丟到同事群組互相取暖
variations: 主管版：「而且桿完還要進烤箱受熱，跟我的 deadline 一模一樣。」
自嘲版：「至少麵團壓完會膨脹，我壓完只會變扁。」

en.content:
Q: Which food has the roughest time at work?
A: Dough.
It gets rolled around all day, morning to night, then scolded for not being flat enough.
Dough sighs: "It's not that I'm lazy — being flattened is just the job description."
en.editorNote: For commiserating in the team chat when overtime has you questioning life
variations: Manager version: "And after the rolling, it goes into the oven—exactly like my deadlines."
Self-deprecating: "At least dough puffs back up after the pressure. I just stay flat."

items.sourceNote: internal only — 台灣諧音梗店名文化重寫成滷味攤對話

zh-tw.content:
夜市滷味攤招牌寫「滷不起」。
我問老闆：「這名字什麼意思？」
老闆：「客人嫌貴的時候，我就指招牌 — 對啊，我也滷（輸）不起。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 跟朋友逛夜市看到怪店名時丟出來，很好接話
variations: 手搖店版：招牌「喝什麼」，客人問要喝什麼，店員直接回 —— 對，喝什麼。
鹹酥雞版：店名「炸咧」，客人問還要等多久，老闆回 —— 就炸咧、炸咧，急什麼。

en.content:
A night-market braised-snack stall had a sign that read "Can't Afford It."
I asked the owner what it meant.
Him: "When customers complain it's pricey, I just point at the sign — yeah, I can't afford it either."
en.editorNote: Toss it out when you spot a weird shop name while wandering a market with friends
variations: Boba version: sign says "What Do You Want." Customer asks what to order. Clerk: "Right, what do you want."
Fried-chicken version: named "Still Frying." Customer asks how long. Owner: "It's still frying, still frying, relax."

items.sourceNote: internal only — 早餐店大冰奶冷笑話重寫成擬人吐槽

zh-tw.content:
問：早餐店的大冰奶為什麼那麼有自信？
答：因為它知道，喝完它的人，接下來一整個上午都會很有「動力」，一直往廁所衝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 早餐群組聊到大冰奶威力時補一刀，大家都有共鳴
variations: 接續版：「所以它不是飲料，是早餐店偷偷發的通腸券。」
溫和版：改成鮮奶茶 ——「至少它讓你早上很專注地做一件事。」

en.content:
Q: Why is the giant iced milk tea at Taiwanese breakfast shops so confident?
A: Because it knows whoever drinks it will have plenty of "motivation" all morning — sprinting straight to the bathroom.
en.editorNote: A follow-up jab when the chat gets going about that drink's legendary effect
variations: Extension: "It's not a beverage, it's a laxative coupon the shop hands out for free."
Milder version with milk tea: "At least it keeps you very focused on one task all morning."

items.sourceNote: internal only — impasta 諧音梗重寫成本土義大利麵店場景

zh-tw.content:
我在義大利麵店打工，客人問：「你們主廚是義大利人嗎？」
我：「不是，他是雲林人。」
客人：「那他做的算正統義大利麵嗎？」
我：「算『冒牌』麵吧 —— 反正客人吃得很開心，麵條也沒抗議。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊到餐廳掛羊頭賣狗肉時，自嘲一下氣氛剛好
variations: 老闆版：「我們最正統的是老闆的口音，一開口就知道很台。」
客人回擊版：「沒關係，反正我吃的是氣氛，不是護照。」

en.content:
I work at a pasta place. A customer asked, "Is your chef actually Italian?"
Me: "Nope, he's from Yunlin."
Customer: "So is it real Italian pasta?"
Me: "More like an impasta — but hey, the customers are happy and the noodles aren't complaining."
en.editorNote: Good for a self-deprecating beat when talking about restaurants overselling their authenticity
variations: Owner version: "The most authentic thing here is the boss's accent — one word and you know he's local."
Customer's comeback: "No worries, I'm here for the vibe, not the passport."

items.sourceNote: internal only — lettuce/brie 蔬果諧音梗重寫成中文冰箱擬人段子

zh-tw.content:
冰箱裡的蔬菜開會，討論誰最沒存在感。
高麗菜：「一定是我，每次都被放到最後才想起來，整顆爛掉。」
九層塔：「不，是我。買回來只用三片，剩下的都陪你天荒地老。」
最角落的薑默默舉手：「各位，我在這裡住三個月了，你們今天才發現我。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 打開冰箱發現忘記的食材時，配這段自我療癒
variations: 結尾加碼：「這時候後面傳來一個聲音 —— 是去年的一包冷凍水餃。」
情侶版：把薑換成「上次分手前買的雙人火鍋料」，笑中帶淚。

en.content:
The vegetables in the fridge held a meeting on who's the most forgotten.
Cabbage: "Definitely me — always remembered last, always rotting whole."
Basil: "No, it's me. You buy me, use three leaves, and I keep you company till the end of time."
The ginger in the far corner quietly raised its hand: "Everyone, I've lived here three months, and you only noticed me today."
en.editorNote: Pair it with that moment you open the fridge and find something you totally forgot
variations: Bonus ending: "Then a voice came from the back — it was a bag of frozen dumplings from last year."
Couple version: swap ginger for 'the hot-pot-for-two kit bought right before the breakup' — bittersweet.

items.sourceNote: internal only — 'seafood diet: I see food and I eat it' 重寫成台灣減肥對話段子

zh-tw.content:
同事說她最近在執行「海鮮飲食法」，超健康。
我問是不是多吃魚少吃肉。
她：「不是，是看到（see）食物我就吃。」
我：「那你昨天的滷味攤呢？」
她：「執行得非常徹底。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 減肥破功那週在群組發，配一張宵夜照最有說服力
variations: 自嘲版：「我也是海鮮飲食法信徒，而且我從不挑食 —— 只要看得到就算數。」
健身教練版：教練說要控制飲食，我說我有，我只吃『看得到的』，看不到的一律不碰。

en.content:
A coworker told me she's on the "seafood diet" — super healthy, she swears.
I asked if that meant more fish, less meat.
Her: "Nope. I see food, I eat it."
Me: "So about that night market run yesterday..."
Her: "Executed flawlessly."
en.editorNote: Post it the week your diet collapses, ideally with a late-night snack photo
variations: Self-roast version: "I'm a devout seafood-diet follower, and I never skip a meal — if I can see it, it counts."
Trainer version: coach said watch what I eat, so I do — I only eat what I can see. Everything else, off the menu.

items.sourceNote: internal only — 'I don't burn calories, I burn toast' 廚藝厭世格式重寫

zh-tw.content:
媽媽問我：「你一個人在外面，都自己煮嗎？」
我：「有啊，我廚藝的巔峰就是把水煮滾。」
媽媽：「那你不是不會燒焦？」
我：「我不燒卡路里，我燒吐司。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 被長輩關心會不會照顧自己時，用這段幽默帶過
variations: 室友版：「我室友的拿手菜是打開外送 App，擺盤還很講究。」
升級版：「別人減肥燃燒脂肪，我減肥靠燃燒鍋子，效果一樣震撼。」

en.content:
Mom asked, "Living on your own, do you actually cook?"
Me: "Sure. My culinary skills peak at boiling water."
Mom: "So at least you never burn anything?"
Me: "I don't burn calories. I burn toast."
en.editorNote: A gentle deflection when relatives ask if you're feeding yourself properly
variations: Roommate version: "My roommate's signature dish is opening the delivery app — and the plating is immaculate."
Upgrade: "Other people burn fat to lose weight. I burn cookware. Equally dramatic results."

items.sourceNote: internal only — 'why did the tomato turn red / saw the salad dressing' 擬人害羞梗改寫成台味蔬菜八卦

zh-tw.content:
冰箱裡番茄突然臉紅了。
小黃瓜問：「你在害羞什麼？」
番茄小聲說：「你沒看到嗎……沙拉旁邊的醬，正在寬衣（換裝）。」
小黃瓜：「那是沙拉醬要淋上來了啦，成熟一點好嗎。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冰箱擬人系列，配一張蔬菜排排站的照片超可愛
variations: 諧音版：番茄說「我不是害羞，我是天生就這麼『紅』，畢竟我是蔬果界的當紅炸子雞。」
辦公室版：把番茄換成新人，臉紅是因為看到暗戀對象走過來，小黃瓜是愛八卦的資深同事。

en.content:
The tomato in the fridge suddenly went bright red.
The cucumber asked, "What are you blushing about?"
The tomato whispered, "Don't you see? The dressing next to the salad is... getting undressed."
Cucumber: "That's just the salad dressing about to pour on. Grow up."
en.editorNote: Part of the anthropomorphic-fridge series — cute with a photo of veggies lined up
variations: Pun version: the tomato says, "I'm not blushing, I'm just naturally this red — I'm the hottest thing in produce, after all."
Office version: swap the tomato for the new hire blushing as their crush walks by, and the cucumber for the gossip-loving senior.

items.sourceNote: internal only — 'impasta' 義大利麵冒充諧音梗改寫成台灣店家對話

zh-tw.content:
客人指著菜單問：「你們這個『正宗手工義大利麵』，主廚真的是義大利人？」
老闆：「不是，他是彰化人。」
客人：「那哪裡正宗？」
老闆：「你看這名字取得多好 —— 一麵（意麵）到底，童叟無欺。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 聊到餐廳浮誇菜名時丟這段，笑點在最後諧音收尾
variations: 升級版：客人再問「那手工的部分呢？」老闆：「手工結帳，一塊都不會少算。」
飲料店版：招牌寫『職人手沖』，老闆坦白：「手是我的沒錯，沖的是三合一。」

en.content:
A customer points at the menu: "Your 'authentic handmade Italian pasta' — is the chef really Italian?"
Owner: "Nope. He's from Changhua."
Customer: "Then what's authentic about it?"
Owner: "Look how honest the name is — it's a real im-pasta, and we never short-change you."
en.editorNote: Drop this when the conversation turns to restaurants with wildly oversold menu names
variations: Upgrade: customer asks, "And the handmade part?" Owner: "Handmade billing — we count every cent by hand."
Drink-shop version: sign says 'artisan hand-poured,' owner admits: "The hands are mine. What I'm pouring is instant mix."
